   292	function findLastIterationRecord(content: string): Record<string, unknown> | null {
   293	  const lines = content.split(/\r?\n/);
   294	  for (let index = lines.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
   295	    const line = lines[index]?.trim();
   296	    if (!line) continue;
   297	    try {
   298	      const parsed = JSON.parse(line);
   299	      if (isObjectRecord(parsed) && parsed.type === CANONICAL_ITERATION_TYPE) {
   300	        return parsed;
   301	      }
   302	    } catch {
   303	    }
   304	  }
   305	  return null;
   306	}
   307	
   308	function validateV2IterationRecord(
   309	  record: Record<string, unknown>,
   310	  deltaIterationRecord: Record<string, unknown> | null,
   311	): V2ValidationFailure[] {
   312	  const failures: V2ValidationFailure[] = [];
   313	  const applicability = record.reviewDepthApplicability;
   314	  const targetSelection = record.targetSelection;
   315	  const searchCoverage = record.searchCoverage;
   316	
   317	  if (!isObjectRecord(applicability)) {
   318	    failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'reviewDepthApplicability must be an object', 'reviewDepthApplicability'));
   319	  } else {
   320	    if (!V2_SCOPE_CLASSES.has(String(applicability.scopeClass))) {
   321	      failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'reviewDepthApplicability.scopeClass must be trivial, standard, or complex', 'reviewDepthApplicability.scopeClass'));
   322	    }
   323	    if (!V2_ENFORCEMENT_MODES.has(String(applicability.enforcement))) {
   324	      failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'reviewDepthApplicability.enforcement must be strict, warn, or skip', 'reviewDepthApplicability.enforcement'));
   325	    }
   326	  }
   327	
   328	  if (!isObjectRecord(targetSelection)) {
   329	    failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'targetSelection must be an object', 'targetSelection'));
   330	  } else {
   331	    if (!Array.isArray(targetSelection.selectedTargets)) {
   332	      failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'targetSelection.selectedTargets must be an array', 'targetSelection.selectedTargets'));
   333	    }
   334	    if (!Array.isArray(targetSelection.discoveryMethods)) {
   335	      failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'targetSelection.discoveryMethods must be an array', 'targetSelection.discoveryMethods'));
   336	    }
   337	  }
   338	
   339	  if (!isObjectRecord(searchCoverage)) {
   340	    failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'searchCoverage must be an object', 'searchCoverage'));
   341	  } else {
   342	    if (!V2_GRAPH_COVERAGE_MODES.has(String(searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode))) {
   343	      failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode must be graph, graphless_fallback, or unavailable_blocked', 'searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode'));
   344	    }
   345	  }
   346	
   347	  const scopeClass = isObjectRecord(applicability) && typeof applicability.scopeClass === 'string'
   348	    ? applicability.scopeClass
   349	    : '';
   350	  const enforcement = isObjectRecord(applicability) && typeof applicability.enforcement === 'string'
   351	    ? applicability.enforcement
   352	    : '';
   353	  const nonTrivialScope = scopeClass !== 'trivial' || enforcement !== 'skip';
   354	
   355	  if (nonTrivialScope) {
   356	    if (!Array.isArray(record.searchLedger) || record.searchLedger.length === 0) {
   357	      failures.push(v2Failure('v2_missing_ledger', 'non-trivial v2 records must include at least one searchLedger row', 'searchLedger'));
   358	    }
   359	
   360	    const findingIds = new Set(
   361	      Array.isArray(record.findingDetails)
   362	        ? record.findingDetails
   363	            .filter(isObjectRecord)
   364	            .map((finding) => (typeof finding.id === 'string' ? finding.id : null))
   365	            .filter((id): id is string => id !== null)
   366	        : [],
   367	    );
   368	
   369	    if (Array.isArray(record.searchLedger)) {
   370	      for (let index = 0; index < record.searchLedger.length; index += 1) {
   371	        const row = record.searchLedger[index];
   372	        if (!isObjectRecord(row)) {
   373	          failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', `searchLedger[${index}] must be an object`, `searchLedger[${index}]`));
   374	          continue;
   375	        }
   376	        const actions = Array.isArray(row.searchActions) ? row.searchActions : [];
   377	        if (
   378	          actions.length === 0
   379	          || actions.some((action) => !isObjectRecord(action) || !isNonEmptyStringArray(action.evidenceRefs))
   380	        ) {
   381	          failures.push(v2Failure('v2_uncited_ledger_row', `searchLedger[${index}] must cite evidenceRefs on every searchActions entry`, `searchLedger[${index}].searchActions`));
   382	        }
   383	        if (row.disposition === 'finding') {
   384	          const linkedFindingId = typeof row.linkedFindingId === 'string' ? row.linkedFindingId : '';
   385	          if (!linkedFindingId || !findingIds.has(linkedFindingId)) {
   386	            failures.push(v2Failure('v2_broken_linked_finding', `searchLedger[${index}] linkedFindingId '${linkedFindingId || '[missing]'}' is not present in findingDetails[].id`, `searchLedger[${index}].linkedFindingId`));
   387	          }
   388	        }
   389	      }
   390	    }
   391	
   392	    if (Array.isArray(record.findingDetails)) {
   393	      for (let index = 0; index < record.findingDetails.length; index += 1) {
   394	        const finding = record.findingDetails[index];
   395	        if (!isObjectRecord(finding)) continue;
   396	        const disposition = typeof finding.disposition === 'string' ? finding.disposition : 'active';
   397	        if (disposition !== 'active') continue;
   398	        if (!isNonEmptyStringArray(finding.affectedSurfaceHints) || typeof finding.scopeProof !== 'string' || finding.scopeProof.trim() === '') {
   399	          const id = typeof finding.id === 'string' ? finding.id : `index ${index}`;
   400	          failures.push(v2Failure('v2_shallow_finding_details', `active finding ${id} must include non-empty scopeProof and affectedSurfaceHints`, `findingDetails[${index}]`));
   401	        }
   402	      }
   403	    }
   404	  }
   405	
   406	  if (deltaIterationRecord) {
   407	    const stateIteration = typeof record.iteration === 'number' ? record.iteration : record.run;
   408	    const deltaIteration = typeof deltaIterationRecord.iteration === 'number'
   409	      ? deltaIterationRecord.iteration
   410	      : deltaIterationRecord.run;
   411	    if (typeof stateIteration === 'number' && typeof deltaIteration === 'number' && stateIteration !== deltaIteration) {
   412	      failures.push(v2Failure(
   413	        'delta_iteration_id_mismatch',
   414	        `state-log iteration ${stateIteration} does not match delta iteration ${deltaIteration}`,
   415	        'iteration',
   416	      ));
   417	    }
   418	  }
   419	
   420	  return failures;
   421	}
   422	
   423	// ───── EXPORTS ─────
   424	
   425	/**
   426	 * Compute a verification confidence score from stage scores.
   427	 *
   428	 * Weighted scoring: compiled (0.35), executed (0.25), testsPassed (0.25),
   429	 * lintClean (0.1), minus autoFixed penalty (0.05).
   430	 *
   431	 * @param stages - Verification stage scores.
   432	 * @returns Confidence score in [0.0, 1.0].
   433	 */
   434	export function computeVerificationConfidence(stages: VerificationStageScores): number {
   435	  let score = 0;
   436	  if (stages.compiled) score += 0.35;
   437	  if (stages.executed) score += 0.25;
   438	  if (stages.testsPassed) score += 0.25;
   439	  if (stages.lintClean) score += 0.1;
   440	  if (stages.autoFixed) score -= 0.05;
   441	  return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, Number(score.toFixed(4))));
   442	}
   443	
   444	/**
   445	 * Run optional verification pass on code blocks extracted from the iteration file.
   446	 *
   447	 * Scans the iteration file for fenced code blocks in configured languages
   448	 * and scores their quality against the threshold.
   449	 *
   450	 * @param iterationFile - Path to the iteration output file.
   451	 * @param recipeConfig - Verification recipe configuration.
   452	 * @returns Pass result with confidence, threshold, and language info.
   453	 */
   454	export function runOptionalVerificationPass(
   455	  iterationFile: string,
   456	  recipeConfig?: PostDispatchRecipeConfig,
   457	): VerificationPassResult {
   458	  if (recipeConfig?.verification_enabled !== true) {
   459	    return { ok: true, skipped: true, reason: 'verification_disabled' };
   460	  }
   461	
   462	  const configuredLanguages = (recipeConfig.verification_languages ?? []).filter(isVerificationLanguage);
   463	  const threshold = recipeConfig.verification_threshold ?? DEFAULT_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD;
   464	  const content = readFileSync(iterationFile, 'utf8');
   465	  const codeBlocks = extractCodeBlocks(content, configuredLanguages);
   466	
   467	  if (codeBlocks.length === 0) {
   468	    return { ok: true, skipped: true, reason: 'no_code_output' };
   469	  }
   470	
   471	  const scoredBlocks = codeBlocks.map((block) => {
   472	    const stages = scoreCodeBlock(block.language, block.code);
   473	    return {
   474	      language: block.language,
   475	      confidence: computeVerificationConfidence(stages),
   476	      stages,
   477	    };
   478	  });
   479	  const lowest = scoredBlocks.reduce((currentLowest, candidate) =>
   480	    candidate.confidence < currentLowest.confidence ? candidate : currentLowest,
   481	  );
   482	
   483	  if (lowest.confidence < threshold) {
   484	    return {
   485	      ok: false,
   486	      reason: 'verification_degraded',
   487	      confidence: lowest.confidence,
   488	      threshold,
   489	      language: lowest.language,
   490	      details: `verification confidence ${lowest.confidence.toFixed(2)} below threshold ${threshold.toFixed(2)}`,
   491	      stages: lowest.stages,
   492	    };
   493	  }
   494	
   495	  return {
   496	    ok: true,
   497	    skipped: false,
   498	    confidence: lowest.confidence,
   499	    threshold,
   500	    language: lowest.language,
   501	    stages: lowest.stages,
   502	  };
   503	}
   504	
   505	/**
   506	 * Validate post-dispatch iteration outputs.
   507	 *
   508	 * Verifies that the JSONL state log was appended, the iteration file exists
   509	 * and is non-empty, required fields are present, and optional v2 depth checks
   510	 * and code verification passes are applied.
   511	 *
   512	 * @param input - Validation input with paths, field requirements, and config.
   513	 * @returns Validation result indicating pass/fail with details.
   514	 */
   515	export function validateIterationOutputs(input: PostDispatchValidateInput): PostDispatchValidateResult {
   516	  const warnings: PostDispatchAdvisory[] = [];
   517	
   518	  repairJsonlTail(input.stateLogPath);
   519	
   520	  if (statSync(input.stateLogPath).size <= input.previousStateLogSize) {
   521	    return {
   522	      ok: false,
   523	      reason: 'jsonl_not_appended',
   524	      details: `no new records since ${input.previousStateLogSize} bytes`,
   525	    };
   526	  }
   527	
   528	  const stateLogContent = readFileSync(input.stateLogPath, 'utf8');
   529	  const lastLine = getLastNonEmptyLine(stateLogContent);
   530	
   531	  try {
   532	    const parsedRecord = JSON.parse(lastLine ?? '');
   533	    if (!isObjectRecord(parsedRecord)) {
   534	      return {
   535	        ok: false,
   536	        reason: 'jsonl_parse_error',
   537	        details: 'Last JSONL line is not an object',
   538	      };
   539	    }
   540	
   541	    if (parsedRecord.type === 'event' && parsedRecord.event === 'dispatch_failure') {
   542	      return {
   543	        ok: false,
   544	        reason: 'dispatch_failure_logged',
   545	        details:
   546	          typeof parsedRecord.reason === 'string'
   547	            ? `dispatch_failure:${parsedRecord.reason}${typeof parsedRecord.detail === 'string' ? `:${parsedRecord.detail}` : ''}`
   548	            : 'dispatch_failure',
   549	      };
   550	    }
   551	
   552	    if (!existsSync(input.iterationFile)) {
   553	      return { ok: false, reason: 'iteration_file_missing', details: input.iterationFile };
   554	    }
   555	
   556	    if (statSync(input.iterationFile).size === 0) {
   557	      return { ok: false, reason: 'iteration_file_empty', details: input.iterationFile };
   558	    }
   559	
   560	    if (parsedRecord.type !== CANONICAL_ITERATION_TYPE) {
   561	      return {
   562	        ok: false,
   563	        reason: 'jsonl_wrong_type',
   564	        details: `last record uses type='${String(parsedRecord.type)}' (expected '${CANONICAL_ITERATION_TYPE}')`,
   565	      };
   566	    }
   567	
   568	    const requiredFields = new Set([
   569	      ...input.requiredJsonlFields,
   570	      ...(
   571	        input.requiredJsonlFields.includes('findingsSummary') || input.requiredJsonlFields.includes('filesReviewed')
   572	          ? REVIEW_ITERATION_FIELDS
   573	          : []
   574	      ),
   575	    ]);
   576	    const missingFields = [...requiredFields].filter((field) => !(field in parsedRecord));
   577	
   578	    if (missingFields.length > 0) {
   579	      return {
   580	        ok: false,
   581	        reason: 'jsonl_missing_fields',
   582	        details: `missing: ${missingFields.join(',')}`,
   583	      };
   584	    }
   585	
   586	    if (requiredFields.has('filesReviewed') && !Array.isArray(parsedRecord.filesReviewed)) {
   587	      return {
   588	        ok: false,
   589	        reason: 'jsonl_missing_fields',
   590	        details: 'filesReviewed must be an array of reviewed file paths',
   591	      };
   592	    }
   593	    if (requiredFields.has('dimensions') && !Array.isArray(parsedRecord.dimensions)) {
   594	      return {
   595	        ok: false,
   596	        reason: 'jsonl_missing_fields',
   597	        details: 'dimensions must be an array',
   598	      };
   599	    }
   600	    if (requiredFields.has('findingDetails') && !Array.isArray(parsedRecord.findingDetails)) {
   601	      return {
   602	        ok: false,
   603	        reason: 'jsonl_missing_fields',
   604	        details: 'findingDetails must be an array',
   605	      };
   606	    }
   607	    if (requiredFields.has('newFindingsRatio') && typeof parsedRecord.newFindingsRatio !== 'number') {
   608	      return {
   609	        ok: false,
   610	        reason: 'jsonl_missing_fields',
   611	        details: 'newFindingsRatio must be a number',
   612	      };
   613	    }
   614	
   615	    if (input.executorKind && input.executorKind !== 'native' && !isObjectRecord(parsedRecord.executor)) {
   616	      return {
   617	        ok: false,
   618	        reason: 'executor_missing',
   619	        details: `missing executor provenance for non-native executor kind '${input.executorKind}'`,
   620	      };

   253	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   254	// 4. CORE LOGIC

   256	
   257	async function main() {
   258	  const args = parseArgs();
   259	  const specFolder = validateNamespaceValue(ensureString(args, 'specFolder'), 'specFolder', inputError);
   260	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   261	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review') {
   262	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research" or "review"');
   263	  }
   264	  const fanoutConfigJson = ensureString(args, 'fanoutConfigJson');
   265	  const baseArtifactDir = ensureString(args, 'baseArtifactDir');
   266	
   267	  const {
   268	    parseFanoutConfig,
   269	    expandLineages,
   270	    resolveCodexSandboxMode,
   271	    resolveClaudePermissionMode,
   272	    resolveGeminiSandboxMode,
   273	    resolveDevinPermissionMode,
   274	  } = await import('../lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts');
   275	
   276	  installSignalHandlers(() => {});
   277	  maybeThrowTestFault();
   278	
   279	  let rawConfig;
   280	  try {
   281	    rawConfig = JSON.parse(fanoutConfigJson);
   282	  } catch {
   283	    throw inputError('fanoutConfigJson is not valid JSON');
   284	  }
   285	
   286	  const fanoutConfig = parseFanoutConfig(rawConfig);
   287	  const allLineages = expandLineages(fanoutConfig);
   288	
   289	  // Fan-out pool handles CLI lineages only; native lineages are dispatched by the YAML step.
   290	  const cliLineages = allLineages.filter((l) => l.kind !== 'native');
   291	
   292	  if (cliLineages.length === 0) {
   293	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no CLI lineages to spawn', results: [], summary: { total: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0, all_failed: false } });
   294	    return;
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  const lineagesDir = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'lineages');
   298	  const ledgerPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-status.log');
   299	  const summaryPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   300	  const runId = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
   301	
   302	  fs.mkdirSync(lineagesDir, { recursive: true });
   303	
   304	  const { results, summary } = await runCappedPool({
   305	    items: cliLineages,
   306	    concurrency: fanoutConfig.concurrency,
   307	    onEvent: (event) => appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, event),
   308	    worker: async (lineage) => {
   309	      const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, lineage.label);
   310	      const stateDir = path.join(lineageDir, '.executor-state');
   311	      fs.mkdirSync(lineageDir, { recursive: true });
   312	      fs.mkdirSync(stateDir, { recursive: true });
   313	
   314	      const sessionId = `fanout-${lineage.label}-${runId}`;
   315	      const prompt = buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage);
   316	
   317	      const resolvedSandbox = resolveCodexSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   318	      const resolvedPermission = resolveClaudePermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   319	      const resolvedGeminiSandbox = resolveGeminiSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   320	      const resolvedDevinPermission = resolveDevinPermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   321	
   322	      const effectiveSandbox = lineage.kind === 'cli-gemini' ? resolvedGeminiSandbox : resolvedSandbox;
   323	      const effectivePermission = lineage.kind === 'cli-devin' ? resolvedDevinPermission : resolvedPermission;
   324	
   325	      const { command, args: cmdArgs, input } = buildLineageCommand(
   326	        lineage,
   327	        prompt,
   328	        effectiveSandbox,
   329	        effectivePermission,
   330	      );
   331	
   332	      // Isolate per-kind state dirs so same-kind replicas do not collide on lockfiles.
   333	      const stateEnvKey = SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND[lineage.kind];
   334	      const extraEnv = {
   335	        SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID: lineage.label,
   336	        ...(stateEnvKey ? { [stateEnvKey]: stateDir } : {}),
   337	      };
   338	
   339	      const timeoutMs = computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage);
   340	
   341	      const result = spawnSync(command, cmdArgs, {
   342	        cwd: process.cwd(),
   343	        encoding: 'utf8',
   344	        timeout: timeoutMs,
   345	        env: { ...process.env, ...extraEnv },
   346	        maxBuffer: 20 * 1024 * 1024,
   347	        ...(typeof input === 'string' ? { input } : {}),
   348	      });
   349	
   350	      // Save subprocess stdout for salvage sweep (write failures in weak CLI executors).
   351	      const logsDir = path.join(lineageDir, 'logs');
   352	      fs.mkdirSync(logsDir, { recursive: true });
   353	      const savedStdout = typeof result.stdout === 'string' ? result.stdout : '';
   354	      fs.writeFileSync(path.join(logsDir, 'fanout-lineage.out'), savedStdout, 'utf8');
   355	
   356	      // Recover missing iteration files from captured stdout when possible.
   357	      const salvage = runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout);
   358	
   359	      const exitCode = result.status ?? (result.error ? 1 : 0);
   360	      return { label: lineage.label, exitCode, timedOut: result.signal === 'SIGTERM', salvage };
   361	    },
   362	  });
   363	
   364	  writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, {
   365	    run_id: runId,
   366	    loop_type: loopType,
   367	    spec_folder: specFolder,
   368	    base_artifact_dir: baseArtifactDir,
   369	    total_cli_lineages: cliLineages.length,
   370	    ...summary,
   371	  });
   372	
   373	  const exitCode = summary.all_failed ? 3 : summary.failed > 0 ? 2 : 0;
   374	  jsonOut({ status: exitCode === 0 ? 'ok' : 'partial', run_id: runId, results, summary });
   375	  process.exit(exitCode);
   376	}
   377

   380	  jsonOut({

   945	            resolved_permission_mode: |

   948	              Mapping: sandboxMode='danger-full-access' → 'dangerous', everything else → 'auto'.

   950	            agent_config_recipe: |
   951	              The review-iter recipe at .opencode/skills/cli-devin/assets/agent-config-deep-review-iter.json
   952	              ships with literal "<repo-root>" and "<packet-root>" placeholders. The command block substitutes
   953	              them via sed at dispatch time and writes the result to {state_paths.prompt_dir}/agent-config-iter-NNN.json,
   954	              then passes that path to devin --agent-config. The recipe pins the narrower allowed_tools
   955	              (Read+Grep+Glob, no Bash) and Read-only permissions.allow at Devin's strict parser. The narrow
   956	              Write scope (v1.0.4.0) is anchored at <packet-root> = {spec_folder} so iterations may only persist
   957	              artifacts inside the current packet directory.
   958	              Contract: .opencode/skills/cli-devin/references/deep-loop-iter-contract.md.

   964	            - "--agent-config pins the review-iter profile (narrower than research-iter — Read+Grep+Glob only, no Bash) at Devin's strict parser.    625	      if (!existsSync(input.deltaFilePath)) {
   626	        return { ok: false, reason: 'delta_file_missing', details: input.deltaFilePath };
   627	      }
   628	      if (statSync(input.deltaFilePath).size === 0) {
   629	        return { ok: false, reason: 'delta_file_empty', details: input.deltaFilePath };
   630	      }

   632	      deltaIterationRecord = findLastIterationRecord(deltaContent);
   633	      if (!deltaIterationRecord) {
   634	        return {
   635	          ok: false,
   636	          reason: 'delta_file_missing_iteration_record',
   637	          details: `${input.deltaFilePath} has no record with type='${CANONICAL_ITERATION_TYPE}'`,

   642	    if (parsedRecord.reviewDepthSchemaVersion !== 2) {
   643	      if (isLegacyNonTrivialReviewRecord(parsedRecord)) {
   644	        warnings.push({
   645	          code: 'legacy_unversioned_record',
   646	          detail: 'non-trivial review record has no reviewDepthSchemaVersion: 2 discriminator; v2 depth checks skipped',
   647	          fieldPath: 'reviewDepthSchemaVersion',
   648	        });
   649	      }
   650	    } else {
   651	      const enforcementMode = getV2EnforcementMode();
   652	      if (enforcementMode === 'off') {
   653	        if (Array.isArray(parsedRecord.searchLedger) && parsedRecord.searchLedger.length > 0) {
   654	          warnings.push({
   655	            code: 'ledger_present_but_unverified',
   656	            detail: 'DEEP_REVIEW_V2_ENFORCEMENT=off skipped v2 searchLedger validation',
   657	            fieldPath: 'searchLedger',
   658	          });
   659	        }
   660	      } else {
   661	        const v2Failures = validateV2IterationRecord(parsedRecord, deltaIterationRecord);
   662	        const identityFailure = v2Failures.find((failure) => failure.reason === 'delta_iteration_id_mismatch');
   663	        if (identityFailure) {
   664	          return {
   665	            ok: false,
   666	            reason: identityFailure.reason,
   667	            details: identityFailure.details,
   668	            warnings: warnings.length > 0 ? warnings : undefined,
   669	          };
   670	        }
   671	        if (v2Failures.length > 0 && enforcementMode === 'strict') {
   672	          return {
   673	            ok: false,
   674	            reason: v2Failures[0].reason,
   675	            details: v2Failures[0].details,
   676	            warnings: warnings.length > 0 ? warnings : undefined,
   677	          };
   678	        }
   679	        if (v2Failures.length > 0) {
   680	          warnings.push({

   682	            detail: 'DEEP_REVIEW_V2_ENFORCEMENT=warn converted v2 depth failures to advisories',
   683	            fieldPath: 'reviewDepthApplicability.enforcement',
   684	          });
   685	          warnings.push(...warningsFromV2Failures(v2Failures));
   686	        }
   687	      }
   688	    }
   689	
   690	    const verificationResult = runOptionalVerificationPass(input.iterationFile, input.recipeConfig);
   691	    if (!verificationResult.ok) {
   692	      appendJsonlRecord(
   693	        input.stateLogPath,
   694	        JSON.parse(buildVerificationDegradedEvent({
   695	          confidence: verificationResult.confidence,
   696	          threshold: verificationResult.threshold,
   697	          language: verificationResult.language,
   698	          details: verificationResult.details,
   699	        })),
   700	      );
   701	      return {
   702	        ok: false,
   703	        reason: 'verification_degraded',
   704	        details: verificationResult.details,
   705	      };
   706	    }
   707	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   708	    const details = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   709	    return { ok: false, reason: 'jsonl_parse_error', details };
   710	  }
   711	
   712	  return warnings.length > 0 ? { ok: true, warnings } : { ok: true };
   713	}
   714	
   715	/**
   716	 * Validate iteration outputs and throw on failure.
   717	 *
   718	 * @param input - Validation input with paths, field requirements, and config.
   719	 * @throws {@link PostDispatchValidationError} If validation fails.
   720	 */
   721	export function validateOrThrow(input: PostDispatchValidateInput): void {
   722	  const result = validateIterationOutputs(input);
   723	
   724	  if (!result.ok) {
   725	    throw new PostDispatchValidationError(result);
   726	  }
   727	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
   200	
   201	/**
   202	 * Build the ordered-results + summary envelope for a completed pool run.
   203	 *
   204	 * @param {Array<Object>} results - Per-item settlement results (ordered).
   205	 * @returns {Object} { results, summary: { total, succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   206	 */
   207	function buildPoolSummary(results) {
   208	  const total = results.length;
   209	  const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result && result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
   210	  const failed = total - succeeded;
   211	  return {
   212	    results,
   213	    summary: {
   214	      total,
   215	      succeeded,
   216	      failed,
   217	      all_failed: total > 0 && failed === total,
   218	    },
   219	  };
   220	}
   221	
   222	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   223	// 4. STATUS LEDGER
   224	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   225	
   226	/**
   227	 * Append one JSONL event to the orchestration status ledger.
   228	 *
   229	 * Generalizes the packet-122 `orchestration-status.log`: one line per lineage
   230	 * lifecycle event (started / completed / failed / salvaged / converged).
   231	 *
   232	 * @param {string} ledgerPath - Path to the JSONL ledger file.
   233	 * @param {Object} entry - A serializable event object.
   234	 */
   235	function appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, entry) {
   236	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(ledgerPath), { recursive: true });
   237	  fs.appendFileSync(ledgerPath, `${JSON.stringify(entry)}\n`);
   238	}
   239	
   240	/**
   241	 * Write the orchestration summary JSON for a completed fan-out run.
   242	 *
   243	 * @param {string} summaryPath - Path to the summary JSON file.
   244	 * @param {Object} summary - Summary payload (e.g. the pool summary plus
   245	 *   salvage counts and per-lineage rollups).
   246	 */
   247	function writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, summary) {
   248	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(summaryPath), { recursive: true });
   249	  fs.writeFileSync(summaryPath, `${JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2)}\n`);
   250	}
   251	
   252	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   253	// 5. EXPORTS
   254	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   255	
   256	module.exports = {
   257	  runCappedPool,
   258	  settleItem,
   259	  buildPoolSummary,
   260	  appendStatusLedger,
   261	  writeOrchestrationSummary,
   262	};

codex
A pattern is emerging: the salvage code is conservative about not inventing iteration numbers, but less conservative about treating arbitrary or partial stdout as a valid markdown artifact. I’m checking whether the surrounding validator would catch those cases or accidentally bless them.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	title: "Fan-out write-failure salvage"
     3	description: "Post-subprocess salvage: recovers missing or empty iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout (opencode --format json text parts or raw fallback), appending a salvaged_from_stdout event or writing a failed-marker placeholder."
     4	---
     5	
     6	# Fan-out write-failure salvage
     7	
     8	---
     9	
    10	## 1. OVERVIEW
    11	
    12	After a CLI lineage subprocess exits, some executors (weak CLIs, sandboxed write
    13	restrictions) may not have written their iteration `.md` files. `runSalvageSweep` reads the
    14	lineage state log to discover which iterations completed (`type == 'iteration'` records),
    15	checks each expected `iteration-N.md`, and for missing or empty files: calls
    16	`extractTextFromOpencodeJson` on the saved stdout (concatenates opencode
    17	`{type:"text",part:{text:...}}` JSONL lines; falls back to raw stdout ≥50 chars), writes the
    18	recovered text and appends a `salvaged_from_stdout` JSONL event to the state log. When no
    19	content is recoverable, writes a `fanout_salvage_failed` HTML comment placeholder.
    20	
    21	### Why This Matters
    22	
    23	Proven mandatory from the packet-122 prototype: weak CLI executors intermittently fail to
    24	write output files. Without salvage, those lineages contribute nothing to the final merge.
    25	
    26	---
    27	
    28	## 2. CURRENT REALITY
    29	
    30	Fully shipped in `fanout-salvage.cjs`. Pure CJS (no TSX bootstrap), exports only —
    31	`runSalvageSweep` and `extractTextFromOpencodeJson` — making it fully testable via
    32	`require()`. `STATE_LOG_BY_LOOP_TYPE` maps `research` → `deep-research-state.jsonl` and
    33	`review` → `deep-review-state.jsonl`.
    34	
    35	---
    36	
    37	## 3. SOURCE FILES
    38	
    39	### Implementation
    40	
    41	| File | Role |
    42	|---|---|
    43	| `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | `extractTextFromOpencodeJson`, `runSalvageSweep`, `STATE_LOG_BY_LOOP_TYPE` |
    44	
    45	### Validation
    46	
    47	| File | Role |
    48	|---|---|
    49	| `tests/unit/fanout-salvage.vitest.ts` | 11 tests: `extractTextFromOpencodeJson` (5: null input, opencode JSON parts, raw fallback, too-short fallback, non-text JSON skipped); `runSalvageSweep` unit (5: no state log, all files present, salvage from opencode stdout, failed marker when no content, mixed present/missing); coverage-graph per-sessionId isolation (1) |
    50	
    51	---
    52	
    53	## 4. SOURCE METADATA
    54	
    55	- Group: Fan-Out
    56	- Feature ID: F026
    57	- Catalog source: `feature_catalog/09--fanout/04-fanout-salvage.md`
    58	- Primary source files: `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs`

    15	Repo root (your workdir): /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public

    18	
    19	THIS ITERATION'S FOCUS:
    20	DIMENSION: fanout-salvage.cjs recovery. Review .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs. Check stdout->md recovery, reuse of post-dispatch-validate, partial/empty output handling, and that salvage never fabricates an iteration that did not run.
    21	
    22	Rules:
    23	- Cite EVERY finding with an exact repo-relative file path and line number. Re-read the cited line before recording a P0.
    24	- Classify each finding: P0 (correctness/security/contradiction that breaks behavior), P1 (degraded/incomplete/missing validation), P2 (style/naming/docs/polish).
    25	- Be skeptical and precise. Do NOT invent line numbers. If you cannot confirm an issue from the actual file, do not report it.
    26	- A clean dimension is a valid result. Report zero findings rather than padding.
    27	
    28	OUTPUT (exactly this structure):
    29	## Focus
    30	<one line: the dimension>
    31	## Findings
    32	<for each: severity, file:line, issue (1-2 sentences), one-line fix. If none: "No findings.">
    33	## Verdict
    34	<one line>
    35	Then a line that is EXACTLY one of: Review verdict: PASS | Review verdict: CONDITIONAL | Review verdict: FAIL
    36	
    37	Then, AFTER the verdict line, emit the machine block:
    38	===FINDINGS_JSON===
    39	[{"id":"f-p123-i4-01","severity":"P0|P1|P2","file":"relative/path","line":123,"issue":"...","fix":"..."}]
    40	===END_FINDINGS===
    41	(emit [] between the markers if there are no findings)

    61	---
    62	name: sk-code-review
    63	description: "Stack-agnostic code-review baseline: findings-first severity, mandatory security/correctness minimums, sk-code evidence."
    64	allowed-tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep]
    65	version: 1.2.0.0
    66	---
    67	
    68	<!-- Keywords: sk-code-review, code-review, pull-request, findings-first, security-review, quality-gate, stack-agnostic, baseline-surface, sk-code -->
    69	
    70	# Code Review Baseline - Stack-Agnostic Findings-First Review
    71	
    72	Universal findings-first review baseline paired with `sk-code` surface standards evidence for the detected code surface.
    73	
    74	## 1. WHEN TO USE
    75	
    76	### Activation Triggers
    77	
    78	Use this skill when:
    79	- A user asks for code review, PR review, quality gate, or merge readiness.
    80	- A workflow dispatches `@review` for pre-commit or gate validation.
    81	- A user requests security/correctness risk analysis before merge.
    82	- A user wants severity-ranked findings with file:line evidence.
    83	
    84	### Keyword Triggers
    85	
    86	`review`, `code review`, `pr review`, `audit`, `security review`, `quality gate`, `request changes`, `findings`, `blocking issues`, `merge readiness`
    87	
    88	### Use Cases
    89	
    90	1. Review-only pass: findings-first output with no code edits.
    91	2. Gate validation: score + pass/fail recommendation for orchestrated workflows.
    92	3. Focused risk pass: security, concurrency, correctness, or removal-focused review.
    93	
    94	### When NOT to Use
    95	
    96	- Feature implementation without review intent.
    97	- Pure documentation editing where code behavior is not being assessed.
    98	- Git-only workflow tasks (branching, rebasing, commit hygiene) without code-quality evaluation intent.
    99	
   100	---
   101	
   102	## 2. SMART ROUTING
   103	
   104	
   105	### Primary Detection Signal
   106	
   107	Review behavior follows a baseline+surface-evidence model:
   108	
   109	- Baseline (always): `sk-code-review` findings-first doctrine.
   110	- Surface standards evidence (when available): `sk-code` detected surface resources.
   111	- Unknown surfaces: review against baseline security/correctness only and disclose uncertainty.
   112	
   113	### Phase Detection
   114	
   115	```text
   116	TASK CONTEXT
   117	    |
   118	    +- STEP 0: Load `sk-code-review` baseline + `sk-code` surface evidence. The dispatcher / agent assembling the sk-code-review prompt MUST prepend `CODE-REVIEW\n\n` as the first two lines of the rendered prompt before the reviewer LLM sees it. Reference resources stay unchanged.
   119	    +- STEP 1: Score intents (top-2 when ambiguity delta <= 1.0)
   120	    +- Phase 1: Scope and baseline checks
   121	    +- Phase 2: Overlay alignment
   122	    +- Phase 3: Findings-first analysis
   123	    +- Phase 4: Output contract and next action
   124	```
   125	
   126	### Resource Domains
   127	
   128	The router discovers markdown resources recursively from `references/` and `assets/`, then applies weighted intent scoring.
   129	
   130	Knowledge is organized by domain mapping:
   131	
   132	```text
   133	references/review_core.md
   134	references/review_ux_single_pass.md
   135	references/*_checklist.md
   136	assets/review/...
   137	```
   138	
   139	- `references/review_core.md` for shared doctrine consumed by both `@review` and `@deep-review`.
   140	- `references/review_ux_single_pass.md` for interactive single-pass review behavior.
   141	- `references/` for baseline review flow, severity contracts, and risk checklists.
   142	- `assets/` for optional reusable templates/checklists (if present in this skill).
   143	
   144	### Resource Loading Levels
   145	
   146	| Level | When to Load | Resources |
   147	| --- | --- | --- |
   148	| ALWAYS | Every invocation, including security/correctness reviews | `references/review_core.md`, `references/review_ux_single_pass.md`, `references/security_checklist.md`, `references/code_quality_checklist.md`, `references/fix-completeness-checklist.md` |
   149	| CONDITIONAL | Intent score indicates need | `references/solid_checklist.md`, `references/code_quality_checklist.md`, `references/removal_plan.md`, `references/test_quality_checklist.md` |
   150	| ON_DEMAND | Explicit deep-dive request | Full mapped reference set |
   151	
   152	### Precedence Matrix
   153	
   154	| Rule Type | Source of Truth | Behavior |
   155	| --- | --- | --- |
   156	| Security/correctness minimums | `sk-code-review` baseline | Always enforced; never relaxed by surface guidance |
   157	| Surface style/process conventions | `sk-code` detected surface | Surface guidance overrides baseline generic style/process advice |
   158	| Verification/build/test commands | `sk-code` detected surface | Surface commands are authoritative for the detected surface |
   159	| Ambiguous conflicts | Escalation | Ask for clarification; do not guess |
   160	
   161	### Unknown Fallback Checklist
   162	
   163	If intent/stack detection is unclear, request:
   164	
   165	1. Review target scope (full diff, staged files, commit range, or explicit file list).
   166	2. Primary risk class (security, correctness, performance, maintainability).
   167	3. Architecture lens priority (KISS/DRY/SOLID strict or optional).
   168	4. Stack/context (system code, web/frontend, or other/full-stack).
   169	5. Desired output mode (findings-only or findings + gated fix follow-up).
   170	
   171	### Smart Router Pseudocode
   172	
   173	```python
   174	from pathlib import Path
   175	
   176	SKILL_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
   177	# Discover resources recursively across references and assets.
   178	RESOURCE_BASES = (SKILL_ROOT / "references", SKILL_ROOT / "assets")
   179	DEFAULT_RESOURCES = [
   180	    "references/review_core.md",
   181	    "references/review_ux_single_pass.md",
   182	    "references/security_checklist.md",
   183	    "references/code_quality_checklist.md",
   184	    "references/fix-completeness-checklist.md",
   185	]
   186	
   187	INTENT_SIGNALS = {
   188	    "SECURITY": {"weight": 5, "keywords": ["security", "auth", "injection", "vulnerability", "race"]},
   189	    "QUALITY": {"weight": 4, "keywords": ["correctness", "bug", "regression", "performance", "boundary", "contract", "breaking change", "backward compatible", "compatibility"]},
   190	    "KISS": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["kiss", "simple", "simplicity", "over-engineer", "overengineering"]},
   191	    "DRY": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["dry", "duplication", "duplicate", "copy-paste", "repeated logic"]},
   192	    "SOLID": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["solid", "architecture", "design", "coupling", "cohesion", "module", "adapter", "interface", "abstraction", "responsibility", "dependency", "boundary"]},
   193	    "REMOVAL": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["remove", "dead code", "cleanup", "deprecate"]},
   194	    "TESTING": {"weight": 3, "keywords": ["test", "tests", "testing", "coverage", "assertion", "mock", "stub", "fixture", "test quality", "brittle"]},
   195	}
   196	
   197	RESOURCE_MAP = {
   198	    "SECURITY": ["references/security_checklist.md"],
   199	    "QUALITY": ["references/code_quality_checklist.md"],
   200	    "KISS": ["references/code_quality_checklist.md"],
   201	    "DRY": ["references/code_quality_checklist.md"],
   202	    "SOLID": ["references/solid_checklist.md"],
   203	    "REMOVAL": ["references/removal_plan.md"],
   204	    "TESTING": ["references/test_quality_checklist.md"],
   205	}
   206	
   207	ON_DEMAND_KEYWORDS = ["deep review", "full review", "all checks", "comprehensive", "flag false positives", "blocking regressions", "list findings", "read-only only", "underrepresented", "scope correctly"]
   208	UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST = [
   209	    "Confirm review scope (diff/staged/files/commit range)",
   210	    "Confirm risk priority (security/correctness/performance/maintainability/test quality/contract safety)",
   211	    "Confirm architecture lens (KISS/DRY/SOLID required or optional)",
   212	    "Confirm stack context (system-code/web/full-stack)",
   213	    "Confirm findings-only vs findings+fix follow-up",
   214	]
   215	
   216	def _task_text(task) -> str:
   217	    return " ".join([
   218	        str(getattr(task, "text", "")),
   219	        str(getattr(task, "query", "")),
   220	        str(getattr(task, "description", "")),
