.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2806:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/review/hs/logs/iter-009.out:43:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780205290663,"sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_function_4vnhn0n98zpe_1","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: deep-loop-runtime\n3: version: 1.4.0\n4: description: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\n5: allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n6: ---\n7: \n8: # Deep Loop Runtime\n9: \n10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n11: \n12: ---\n13: \n14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE\n15: \n16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n17: \n18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n19: \n20: - executor configuration and audit metadata\n21: - prompt-pack rendering\n22: - post-dispatch validation\n23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair\n24: - loop locking and permissions gates\n25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing\n26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation\n27: \n28: ### Activation Triggers\n29: \n30: This skill is activated when:\n31: \n32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`\n33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`\n34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts\n35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing\n36: \n37: ### When NOT to Use\n38: \n39: Do not invoke this skill when:\n40: \n41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)\n42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)\n43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)\n44: \n45: ---\n46: \n47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING\n48: \n49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.\n50: \n51: ### Primary Detection Signal\n52: \n53: ```bash\n54: request_text=\"$(printf '%s' \"$USER_REQUEST\" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')\"\n55: case \"$request_text\" in\n56:   *\"deep-loop runtime\"*|*\"deep-loop-runtime\"*|*\"executor config\"*|*\"prompt pack\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n57:   *\"coverage graph\"*|*\"deep_loop_graph\"*|*\"bayesian scorer\"*|*\"loop lock\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n58:   *\"fallback router\"*|*\"post-dispatch validate\"*|*\"jsonl repair\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;\n60: esac\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Phase Detection\n64: \n65: ```text\n66: TASK CONTEXT\n67:     |\n68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent\n69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs\n70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree\n71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ### Resource Domains\n75: \n76: ```text\n77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)\n78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules\n79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)\n80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)\n81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility\n82: ```\n83: \n84: ### Resource Loading Levels\n85: \n86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |\n87: |------|-------------|---------|\n88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |\n89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |\n90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |\n91: \n92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode\n93: \n94: ```text\n95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. \"bayesian-scorer\", \"prompt-pack\"):\n96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts\n97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:\n98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts\n99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):\n100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts\n101: ELSE:\n102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)\n103: ```\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS\n108: \n109: ### Runtime Architecture\n110: \n111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).\n112: \n113: ```text\n114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]\n115:             │\n116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)\n117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite\n118:             │\n119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)\n120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.\n121: ```\n122: \n123: ### Library Structure\n124: \n125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**\n126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings\n127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)\n128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template\n129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)\n130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes\n131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines\n132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking\n133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks\n134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring\n135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix\n136: \n137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**\n138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle\n139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders\n140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction\n141: \n142: ### Council Primitives\n143: \n144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.\n145: \n146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**\n147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.\n148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.\n149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.\n150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.\n151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.\n152: \n153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.\n154: \n155: ### Script Entry Points\n156: \n157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):\n158: \n159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |\n160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|\n161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |\n166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |\n167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |\n168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |\n169: \n170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.\n171: \n172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.\n173: \n174: ### Test Organization\n175: \n176: ```text\n177: tests/\n178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)\n179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures\n180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification\n181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests\n182: ```\n183: \n184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.\n185: \n186: ---\n187: \n188: ## 4. RULES\n189: \n190: ### ALWAYS\n191: \n192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)\n193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)\n194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)\n195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)\n196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write\n197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation\n198: \n199: ### NEVER\n200: \n201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction\n202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection\n203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead\n204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`\n205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix\n206: \n207: ### ESCALATE IF\n208: \n209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override\n210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR\n211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 5. SCOPE\n217: \n218: In scope:\n219: \n220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`\n221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`\n222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`\n223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`\n224: - runtime tests under `tests/`\n225: \n226: Out of scope:\n227: \n228: - MCP tool registration\n229: - MCP handler ownership\n230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`\n231: - direct user command routing\n232: \n233: ---\n234: \n235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE\n236: \n237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.\n238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.\n239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.\n240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.\n241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.\n242: \n243: Shipped layout:\n244: \n245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |\n246: |------|--------------|---------|\n247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |\n248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |\n249: | `scripts/` | 4 files | Direct `status`, `query`, `upsert`, and `convergence` entry points replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools. |\n250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\n251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS\n256: \n257: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.\n258: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.\n259: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.\n260: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.\n261: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.\n262: \n263: ---\n264: \n265: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA\n266: \n267: The runtime is healthy when:\n268: \n269: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract\n270: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors\n271: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n272: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob\n273: \n274: ---\n275: \n276: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES\n277: \n278: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.\n279: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.\n280: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.\n281: \n282: Related resources:\n283: \n284: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points\n285: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`\n286: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory\n\n(End of file - total 286 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: deep-loop-runtime\nversion: 1.4.0\ndescription: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\nallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n---\n\n# Deep Loop Runtime\n\nShared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n\n---\n\n## 1. WHEN TO USE\n\nThis skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n\nUse it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n\n- executor configuration and audit metadata","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","time":{"start":1780205290639,"end":1780205290662}},"id":"prt_e7c81029a0018WgctXtXLcmFh5","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80eee8001ZiIO6I4pjCr85X"}}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3160:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/005-resilience-and-advisor/001-code-graph-advisor-refinement/research/001-code-graph-advisor-refinement-roadmap/deltas/iter-002.jsonl:1:{"type":"iteration","iteration":2,"newInfoRatio":0.86,"status":"complete","focus":"RQ-01 depth + RQ-03 depth + RQ-04 groundwork","noveltyJustification":"7 depth findings (F8-F14); 5 fully novel (F8/F9/F10/F11/F12), 2 refine+extend iter 1 (F13/F14 weight 0.5 each) = 6.0/7 ≈ 0.857","questionsAddressed":["RQ-01","RQ-03","RQ-04"],"questionsAnswered":["RQ-01-depth","RQ-03-depth"],"ruledOut":["hypothesis: tree-sitter has call_expression query but it is bypassed — FALSE, structurally absent","hypothesis: semantic-lock.ts provides concurrency locking — FALSE, it is a weight-gate assertion"],"timestamp":"2026-04-24T22:00:00Z"}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3243:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/005-resilience-and-advisor/001-code-graph-advisor-refinement/research/001-code-graph-advisor-refinement-roadmap/deep-research-state.jsonl:3:{"type":"iteration","iteration":2,"newInfoRatio":0.86,"status":"complete","focus":"RQ-01 depth (CALLS structurally regex-only) + RQ-03 depth (5-state trust-state, INV-F5 concurrency exposures) + RQ-04 groundwork (two-cycle magnitude-blind, 3/12 gates typed boolean)","graphEvents":[{"type":"node","id":"code-graph/lib/tree-sitter-parser.ts","label":"AST walker — has NO call_expression capture"},{"type":"node","id":"skill-advisor/lib/freshness/trust-state.ts","label":"5-state machine (unavailable > absent > stale > live)"},{"type":"node","id":"skill-advisor/lib/freshness/cache-invalidation.ts","label":"event-bus invalidator (no lock, 3 race exposures)"},{"type":"node","id":"skill-advisor/lib/promotion/semantic-lock.ts","label":"weight-gate (NOT a concurrency lock)"},{"type":"node","id":"skill-advisor/lib/promotion/two-cycle-requirement.ts","label":"magnitude-blind binary accumulator"},{"type":"edge","id":"E8","relation":"structural_gap","source":"code-graph/lib/tree-sitter-parser.ts","target":"code-graph/lib/structural-indexer.ts::CALLS-regex"},{"type":"edge","id":"E9","relation":"overrides_provenance","source":"code-graph/lib/structural-indexer.ts::CALLS","target":"code-graph/lib/tree-sitter-parser.ts::detectorProvenance"},{"type":"edge","id":"E10","relation":"priority_branch","source":"skill-advisor/lib/freshness/trust-state.ts::createTrustState","target":"unavailable>absent>stale>live"},{"type":"edge","id":"E11","relation":"captures_but_ignores","source":"skill-advisor/lib/promotion/shadow-cycle.ts::deltaVsLive","target":"skill-advisor/lib/promotion/two-cycle-requirement.ts::consecutivePasses"},{"type":"edge","id":"E12","relation":"typed_boolean","source":"skill-advisor/lib/promotion/gate-bundle.ts::paritySlices","target":"ambiguityStable/derivedLaneAttributionComplete/stuffingRejected"}]}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3284:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/001-local-embeddings-foundation/016-llama-cpp-retrieval-quality-probe/scratch/probe-corpus.json:455:    "content": "---\ntitle: \"...it/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-runtime-executor-hardening/001-foundational-runtime/implementation-summary]\"\ndescription: \"Phase 017 complete: 25 commits shipped to main closing all 10 original review P1 findings + 9 new segment-2 P1 findings + 10 P2 refactors. Verdict: CONDITIONAL → PASS. Headline: H-56-1 canonical save metadata no-op eliminated. Two new clusters remediated: D (code-graph 6:1 sibling asymmetry) and E (Copilot runtime observability gap).\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"017 implementation summary\"\n  - \"phase 017 summary\"\n  - \"review findings remediation summary\"\n  - \"h-56-1 fix\"\n  - \"canonical save metadata\"\nimportance_tier: \"critical\"\ncontextType: \"implementation\"\ntemplate_source_hint: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: impl-summary-core | v2.2 -->\"\n_memory:\n  continuity:\n    packet_pointer: \"system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-runtime-executor-hardening/001-foundational-runtime\"\n    last_updated_at: \"2026-04-17T18:00:00Z\"\n    last_updated_by: \"claude-opus-4.7\"\n    recent_action: \"Phase 017 implementation complete: 25 commits landed on main across 4 waves (A=5, B=10, C=4, D=3) + 3 support commits. All 27 tasks closed. Verdict CONDITIONAL → PASS with hasAdvisories=true for 8+3 parking-lot P2 items deferred to Phase 019.\"\n    next_safe_action: \"Draft v3.4.0.2 release + optional /deep:start-review-loop :auto on Phase 017 scope for final ship gate (if desired beyond smoke-test validation already performed). Phase 018 autonomous Copilot iteration unblocked.\"\n    blockers: []\n    completion_pct: 100\n    key_files:\n      - \"spec.md\"\n      - \"plan.md\"\n      - \"tasks.md\"\n      - \"checklist.md\"\n      - \"implementation-summary.md\"\n    session_dedup:\n      fingerprint: \"sha256:phase-017-remediation-complete-25-commits\"\n      session_id: \"017-implementation-2026-04-17\"\n      parent_session_id: \"016-foundational-runtime/001-initial-research\"\n    open_questions: []\n    answered_questions:\n      - \"H-56-1 canonical save metadata no-op\"\n      - \"Copilot compact-cache parity requirement\"\n      - \"MCP transport-layer caller identity mechanism\"\n      - \"trustState vocabulary alignment\"\n      - \"EVIDENCE marker false-positive detection strategy\"\n---\n# Implementation Summary: Phase 017 Review-Findings Remediation\n\n<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 2 -->\n<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: impl-summary-core | v2.2 -->\n<!-- HVR_REFERENCE: .opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/hvr_rules.md -->\n\n> **Status: COMPLETE.** Phase 017 shipped 25 commits to `main` on 2026-04-17, closing all 27 remediation tasks from the consolidated backlog (10 original review P1 + 9 new segment-2 P1 + 10 P2 refactors). Headline: **H-56-1 canonical save metadata no-op eliminated** via 2-line fix in `workflow.ts` + 38-folder lastUpdated cascade. Verdict transitions from **CONDITIONAL → PASS** with `hasAdvisories=true` for 8+3 parking-lot P2 items deferred to Phase 019.\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:metadata -->\n## Metadata\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Spec Folder** | `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-runtime-executor-hardening/001-foundational-runtime/` |\n| **Parent Packet** | `026-graph-and-context-optimization` |\n| **Started** | 2026-04-17 14:04 (spec folder scaffold) |\n| **Completed** | 2026-04-17 18:00 (Wave D Cluster D3 merged) |\n| **Level** | 2 |\n| **Stage** | COMPLETE — all 4 waves (A/B/C/D) shipped |\n| **Review Source** | `../review/016-foundational-runtime-pt-01/review-report.md` (7-iteration deep-review, verdict CONDITIONAL, 10 P1 + 18 P2) |\n| **Segment-2 Source** | `../research/016-foundational-runtime-pt-01/segment-2-synthesis.md` (7-iteration Opus meta-research, 14 new findings, 3 compound hypotheses) |\n| **Tasks Closed** | 27 (10 P1 review + 9 P1 segment-2 + 8 P2 Wave D) |\n| **Commits to Main** | 25 (Wave A=5, Wave B=10, Wave C=4, Wave D=3, Support=3) |\n| **Architectural Primitives Added** | 4 (AsyncLocalStorage caller-context, readiness-contract module, shared-provenance module, retry-budget) |\n| **New Test Files** | 16 vitest suites (~280 test cases total) |\n| **Effort Budget (planned)** | ~105h (3-engineer parallelized) / ~60h critical-path |\n| **Effort Actual (shipped)** | ~6 wall-clock hours via parallel cli-copilot gpt-5.4 high (3 concurrent max per `feedback_copilot_concurrency_override`) |\n| **Executor CLI** | cli-copilot gpt-5.4 --effort high (primary); Opus 4.7 (Wave A orchestration + finalization) |\n<!-- /ANCHOR:metadata -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:what-built -->\n## What Was Built\n\nPhase 017 closed the remediation surface surfaced by the deep-review of v3.4.0.1 (10 P1 + 18 P2 findings, verdict CONDITIONAL) plus 14 additional findings from a 7-iteration Opus meta-research pass. The dominant discovery was **H-56-1**: the default `/memory:save` canonical-save path was structurally a metadata-freshness no-op. Not drift — by design, since a dead-code guard shipped. The fix cascaded through four waves of infrastructure hardening.\n\n### Four architectural primitives introduced\n\n**Primitive 1 — Canonical save writes metadata.** `scripts/core/workflow.ts:1259` no longer hardcodes `ctxFileWritten = false`. The 70-LOC description.json tracking block (lines 1261-1331) is now reachable and writes `lastUpdated: new Date().toISOString()` on every canonical save. `workflow.ts:1333` no longer gates `refreshGraphMetadata` on `plannerMode === 'full-auto'` — the follow-ups now run unconditionally. Net effect: every `/memory:save` invocation refreshes `description.json.lastUpdated` + `graph-metadata.json.derived.*`. Atomic-shipped in commit `aaf0f49a8` (T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04).\n\n**Primitive 2 — Code-graph readiness contract.** New `mcp_server/lib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts` (commit `4a154c555`) exports 4 helpers previously inline in `query.ts`: `canonicalReadinessFromFreshness`, `queryTrustStateFromFreshness`, `buildQueryGraphMetadata`, `buildReadinessBlock`. Types re-exported: `ReadyResult`, `StructuralReadiness`, `SharedPayloadTrustState` (the canonical 8-state vocabulary from `lib/context/shared-payload.ts:36`). Propagated to 5 sibling handlers in commit `f253194bf` (T-W1-CGC-03): `scan.ts`, `status.ts`, `context.ts`, `ccc-status.ts`, `ccc-reindex.ts`, `ccc-feedback.ts`. CCC trio ships stub rollout (`trustState: 'unavailable'` + `reason: 'readiness_not_applicable'`) where full-readiness semantics don't apply.\n\n**Primitive 3 — MCP caller context (AsyncLocalStorage).** New `mcp_server/lib/context/caller-context.ts` (commit `debb5d7a8`) exports `runWithCallerContext<T>`, `getCallerContext`, `requireCallerContext`. Transport wraps each request dispatch in `context-server.ts` with caller identity (sessionId + transport type + connectedAt + callerPid + metadata). Handlers access via `getCallerContext()` at the boundary — zero signature churn. 12 tests cover propagation across `await`, `setTimeout`, `Promise.all`, nested runs, readonly immutability. Consumed by T-SRS-BND-01 (commit `87636d923`) which rejects mismatched `args.sessionId` in `handleSessionResume`.\n\n**Primitive 4 — Retry budget.** New `mcp_server/lib/enrichment/retry-budget.ts` (commit `61f93c9bf`) tracks `(memoryId, step, reason)` tuples with attempt counts. `shouldRetry` skips after N=3 failures; `recordFailure` updates state; `clearBudget(memoryId?)` resets per-memory or globally. Consumed by `handlers/save/post-insert.ts` for `partial_causal_link_unresolved` outcomes — no more unbounded retry loops on structurally non-retryable references.\n\n### Cluster B — Canonical save surface hygiene (7 tasks)\n\nBeyond the workflow.ts fix, this cluster covered:\n- Research folder backfill (`88063287b` T-CNS-02): walks `research/NNN-*/iterations/` and auto-creates missing `description.json` + `graph-metadata.json`\n- Continuity freshness validator (`32a180bba` T-W1-CNS-05): new `scripts/validation/continuity-freshness.ts` wired into `validate.sh --strict`. Warns when `_memory.continuity.last_updated_at` lags `graph-metadata.derived.last_save_at` by >10 minutes\n- Context error branch (`db36c3194` T-CGC-02): `handlers/code-graph/context.ts` replaces silent catch with explicit `reason: 'readiness_check_crashed'` surfacing via 4-state `trustState: 'unavailable'`\n- Design-intent comments (`f42c5d3b6` T-RBD-03): documents intentional rollup divergence between `post-insert.ts` executionStatus (failure-with-recovery semantics) and `response-builder.ts` postInsertEnrichment (MCP-client-facing coarser status)\n- Evidence-marker lint strict activation (`e40dff0bb` T-EVD-01): `scripts/validation/evidence-marker-lint.ts` wraps the bracket-depth parser for `validate.sh --strict` mode\n- 16-folder canonical-save sweep (`176bad2b2` T-CNS-03): one-time `lastUpdated` refresh across 11 stale/missing folders. Combined with H-56-1 cascade (`8859da9cd`) which already refreshed 7 others, all 17 sibling 026-tree folders now fresh\n\n### Cluster D — Code-graph sibling asymmetry (NEW — 1 task)\n\nThe deep-review captured only `context.ts` as un-hardened relative to `query.ts`. Segment-2 iter 52 revealed the full asymmetry was 6:1 — all 5 other siblings also missed the readiness vocabulary. Fix collapsed into `f253194bf` (T-W1-CGC-03) via Primitive 2 above.\n\n### Cluster E — Runtime-specific observability gap (NEW — 2 tasks)\n\nCompound hypothesis H-56-4 from segment-2 iter 56 confirmed Copilot hook runtime was the only one of three missing compact-cache. Fix:\n- `77da3013a` (T-W1-HOK-02): extracted `hooks/shared-provenance.ts` from `hooks/claude/shared.ts`. All three runtimes now import from one canonical source. Broke Gemini's transitive `../claude/shared.js` coupling.\n- `5923737c7` (T-W1-HOK-01): new `hooks/copilot/compact-cache.ts` + extended `session-prime.ts`. Copilot now writes and reads `trustState: 'cached'` matching Claude/Gemini.\n\n### Cluster A — Scope normalizer drift (2 tasks)\n\nThe 5-way duplication was a latent correctness landmine. Fix:\n- `b923623cc` (T-SCP-01): new `normalizeScopeValue(value: unknown): string | null` canonical export in `lib/governance/scope-governance.ts`. 4 local duplicates collapsed (`reconsolidation-bridge.ts:228`, `lineage-state.ts:198`, `save/types.ts:348`, `preflight.ts:440`). Per-callsite null-semantics audit preserved — canonical returns `string | null` (not `undefined`) to match existing consumer narrowing.\n- `ded5ece07` (T-SCP-02): lint rule rejects new `normalizeScope*` / `getOptionalString` declarations outside `scope-governance.ts`.\n\n### Cluster C — ASCII-only sanitization (1 P1 + 3 P2 tasks, atomic-shipped)\n\nCommit `1bd7856a9` (T-SAN-01 + T-SAN-02 + T-SAN-03) landed three changes together:\n- `shared/gate-3-classifier.ts:normalizePrompt` — `.normalize('NFKC')` + strip `[\\u00AD\\u200B-\\u200F\\uFEFF]` before `.toLowerCase()`. Cyrillic `е` + zero-width/soft-hyphen no longer bypass file-write detection.\n- `hooks/claude/shared.ts:sanitizeRecoveredPayload` — mirror NFKC pass. Greek `Ε` (U+0395) no longer injects `SYST\\u0395M:` past the system-role-prefix regex.\n- New `scripts/tests/gate-3-classifier.vitest.ts` — 5+ unicode cases (Cyrillic `е`, soft hyphen, zero-width space, Greek `Ε`, combined).\n\n### Standalones (4 tasks)\n\n- `87636d923` (T-SRS-BND-01): session-resume auth binding via caller-context. `MCP_SESSION_RESUME_AUTH_MODE=permissive` env flag for canary rollout. Default = strict (reject).\n- `0c9d6f612` (T-CPN-01): closing-pass CP-002 marked RESOLVED by commit `e774eef07` (T-PIN-08). Wave C agent caught CP-002 actually referred to `graph-lifecycle.ts:onIndex()` skip-reason collapse, not the entity-linker fallback — docs updated truthfully.\n- `ad02986fe` (T-W1-MCX-01): `StructuralRoutingNudgeMeta.readiness` renamed to `advisoryPreset`. Always-literal `'ready'` — name implied dynamism that didn't exist.\n- `b26514cbc` (T-YML-CP4-01): CP-004 YAML predicate — moved prose timing from `when:` to canonical `after:` per Wave D agent's judgment. The statement was timing metadata, not a BooleanExpr.\n\n### Wave D P2 maintainability (6 tasks)\n\n- `787bf4f88` (T-EXH-01 + T-W1-PIN-02): `lib/utils/exhaustiveness.ts` with `assertNever` helper applied to 8 typed unions. `satisfies Record<OnIndexSkipReason, EnrichmentSkipReason>` clause at post-insert.ts:302.\n- `0ac9cdcba` (T-PIN-GOD-01 + T-RCB-DUP-01): `runEnrichmentStep<T>` helper reduces `runPostInsertEnrichment` from 243 LOC → 32 LOC. `runAtomicReconsolidationTxn` extracted from `executeConflict` duplicate transaction blocks.\n- `b26514cbc` (T-W1-HST-02): new `DEPLOYMENT.md` at repo root documenting Docker `-v /tmp:/tmp` anti-pattern (R53-P1w-001), Copilot runtime notes, and `MCP_SESSION_RESUME_AUTH_MODE` flag.\n\n### EVIDENCE audit (Wave A prep)\n\nCommit `7d85861a0` added `scripts/validation/evidence-marker-audit.ts` — bracket-depth parser (not regex) that distinguishes real unclosed `[EVIDENCE:...]` markers from paren-in-content false positives. Skips fenced code blocks and inline backtick spans. Scan results: 1962 markers across 16 folders; ~210 genuinely malformed (mostly in 016 checklist + plan + tasks); rewrapped in-place. Future going forward enforced by T-EVD-01 lint.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:what-built -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:how-delivered -->\n## How It Was Delivered\n\n### Dispatch model\n\nPer `feedback_phase_018_autonomous.md` + `feedback_copilot_concurrency_override.md`:\n- **Primary executor**: cli-copilot gpt-5.4 with `--effort high` + `--allow-all-tools --allow-all-paths`\n- **Concurrency cap**: 3 agents at a time (GitHub Copilot API throttle)\n- **Orchestration**: Opus 4.7 (this session) for task sequencing, atomic-ship verification, final synthesis\n- **Model for Wave A**: Opus 4.7 via Task tool subagents (critical-path infrastructure; higher fidelity)\n- **Model for Waves B/C/D**: cli-copilot gpt-5.4 high (parallel lanes; user-directed executor)\n\n### Wave execution order\n\n**Wave A — Infrastructure Primitives (2 sub-waves of 3 concurrent agents each, ~20h budget)**\n\nWave A1 (parallel, 3 Opus agents): T-CNS-01+04 merged-PR agent, T-CGC-01 readiness extract, T-W1-HOK-02 shared-provenance extract.\nWave A2 (parallel, 2 agents): T-SCP-01 normalizer collapse, EVIDENCE audit bracket-depth parser.\n\nAtomic-ship verification for T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04: merged-PR agent checked `git show HEAD | grep -c 'workflow.ts'` = 1 commit touching both lines 1259 AND 1333.\n\n**Wave A Smoke Gate**: `tsc --noEmit` clean + 49/49 Wave A test files + `workflow-memory-tracking.vitest.ts` regression suite + `graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts` — all green. No regressions detected. Full `/deep:start-review-loop :auto` ×7 deferred to optional final ship gate.\n\n**Wave B — Cluster Consumers (4 parallel lanes, ~30h effective wall-clock)**\n\n- Lane B1 (12h): T-CNS-02, T-W1-CNS-05, T-CGC-02, T-RBD-03 (4 tasks, 4 commits)\n- Lane B2 (16h): T-W1-CGC-03, T-W1-HOK-01 (2 tasks, 2 commits). CCC trio stub-rollout strategy used for `ccc-status/reindex/feedback` where full readiness doesn't apply.\n- Lane B3 (12h): T-SCP-02, T-SAN-01/02/03 atomic, T-PIN-RET-01 (3 commits, atomic-shipped NFKC trio).\n- Lane B4a (24h): MCP caller-context AsyncLocalStorage (1 commit, integrated into context-server.ts CallToolRequestSchema handler).\n- Lane B4b (16h, sequential after B4a): session-resume auth binding (1 commit, 8/8 auth tests + 29/29 regression slice green).\n\n**Wave B Light Gate**: targeted vitest + tsc per commit; broader cross-lane sweep after merge (145/145 tests green for code-graph + hooks + handler-save slice).\n\n**Wave C — Rollout + Sweeps (~15h, 4 commits)**\n\n- T-EVD-01 lint strict activation + T-CPN-01 CP-002 amend + T-W1-MCX-01 readiness rename (3 commits via one cli-copilot agent).\n- T-CNS-03 16-folder lastUpdated sweep executed directly via jq loop (mechanical task, no LLM reasoning needed). Smoke-tested on `001-research-graph-context-systems` (4-day-stale) → successful fresh update → proceeded sweep on 11 stale/missing folders.\n\n**Wave D — P2 Maintainability (~40h budget, 3 commits via one cli-copilot agent)**\n\nSingle agent ran through all 3 clusters serially with per-cluster commits:\n- D1 Typing (T-EXH-01 + T-W1-PIN-02)\n- D2 Extraction (T-PIN-GOD-01 + T-RCB-DUP-01)\n- D3 Predicates + Docs (T-YML-CP4-01 + T-W1-HST-02)\n\n### Parallel-lane collision avoidance\n\nAll 4 Wave B lanes ran concurrently without file-level conflicts:\n- Lane B1: `scripts/memory/`, `scripts/validation/`, `handlers/code-graph/context.ts`, `handlers/save/post-insert.ts` (comments only)\n- Lane B2: `handlers/code-graph/{scan,status,ccc-*}.ts`, `hooks/copilot/`\n- Lane B3: `shared/gate-3-classifier.ts`, `hooks/claude/shared.ts`, `handlers/save/post-insert.ts` (retry-budget only — not same regions as B1)\n- Lane B4a: `lib/context/caller-context.ts` (new), `context-server.ts`\n- Lane B4b (sequential, not parallel): `handlers/session-resume.ts`\n\nPost-insert.ts was the one shared file across B1 (T-RBD-03 design comments on rollup sites), B3 (T-PIN-RET-01 retry budget integration in `partial_causal_link_unresolved` path), and D2 (T-PIN-GOD-01 god-function extract). Landed in 3 separate commits across 3 waves without merge conflicts.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:how-delivered -->\n\n---\n\n## Unattributed Support + Finalization Commits\n\nThese commits landed during Phase 017 but were not tied to one child-wave task row, so they stay tracked here as packet-level support and delivery evidence.\n\n### Support commits\n\n- `8541e2e3c` — packet scaffold for the parent and four child phase folders.\n- `8859da9cd` — H-56-1 cascade refresh across the 026 tree while the canonical-save fix was being exercised in live packet work.\n- `fc06d2023` — Phase 018→017 rename cleanup plus the segment-2 research artifact landing that stabilized the packet naming and source references.\n\n### Finalization commits\n\n- `b308333d2` — initial `v3.4.0.2` changelog draft plus the parent Phase 017 implementation summary.\n- `2185eb10f` — sk-doc alignment sweep across the Phase 017 artifacts and adjacent policy-facing docs.\n- `9ebd9b41c` — per-wave implementation summaries plus the 026 root packet refresh.\n- `95d2e80b9` — feature catalog and manual testing playbook entries for the Phase 017 surfaces.\n- `6637c86f5` — changelog restyle to match the v3.4.0.1 presentation format.\n\n### Post-push follow-ups\n\n- `48fc7db91` — sk-code-opencode standards alignment pass across the shipped Phase 017 files.\n- `a3200d1bd` — post-push normalizer-lint false-positive fix excluding `dist/` and `node_modules/`.\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:decisions -->\n## Key Decisions\n\n### Scope adjustments made before implementation began\n\n1. **Copilot compact-cache FULL PARITY**: After Explore agent raised Copilot's hook architecture is deliberately minimal, user chose full parity with Claude/Gemini because Phase 018 autonomous execution plans cli-copilot as primary.\n2. **Session-resume auth EXPANDED SCOPE 8h → 40h**: Explore confirmed MCP transport-layer caller identity didn't exist. Implemented via AsyncLocalStorage rather than signature propagation (zero handler churn — collapsed Lane B4b scope).\n3. **EVIDENCE audit AUDIT+REWRAP ALL**: Bracket-depth parser rather than regex to distinguish real unclosed markers from paren-in-content false positives.\n4. **trustState vocabulary 8-STATE ALIGNED**: Used existing `SharedPayloadTrustState` canonical type rather than creating 4-state enum. Zero type proliferation.\n\n### Implementation-time decisions\n\n- **Deep-review ×7 rebalancing**: Plan specified ×7 per wave (28 iterations total). Actual execution used smoke-gate (tsc + targeted vitest) per wave instead. Saved ~40h of agent time. Full ×7 remains available as optional final ship gate.\n- **T-CNS-03 jq loop vs cli-copilot**: The 16-folder sweep is a mechanical `lastUpdated` refresh — no LLM reasoning needed. Executed directly via shell loop.\n- **CCC trio stub rollout**: `ccc-status`, `ccc-reindex`, `ccc-feedback` don't have readiness semantics in the same shape as `query.ts`. Emit `trustState: 'unavailable'` + `reason: 'readiness_not_applicable'` stub rather than forcing full readiness.\n- **CP-004 as `after:` timing, not `when:` BooleanExpr**: Wave D agent caught that the prose at `spec_kit_complete_confirm.yaml:1099` was timing metadata (post-event narration), not a boolean predicate. Moved to canonical `after:` per `boolean-expr.ts` grammar note.\n- **Phase 018→017 rename disambiguation**: Historical \"Phase 017\" references (27 v3.4.0.1 remediation commits) renamed to \"Phase 016 remediation\" in 016/ docs to disambiguate from the new 017/ folder. New-work references (017 folder) keep \"Phase 017\" label.\n\n### Atomic-ship groups respected\n\n- T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04: single PR on `workflow.ts` (no transient inconsistency window)\n- T-SAN-01 + T-SAN-02 + T-SAN-03: single commit (tests fail until code lands)\n- T-SCP-01 with all 4 callers: single commit\n- T-CGC-01 + T-W1-CGC-03: landed staged with stub-rollout strategy (not atomic — CCC trio uses stub variant)\n- T-W1-HOK-02 before T-W1-HOK-01: order preserved (Wave A before Wave B so Copilot compact-cache imports from already-extracted shared-provenance)\n<!-- /ANCHOR:decisions -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:verification -->\n## Verification\n\n### Per-commit validation (25 commits)\n\n- **TypeScript compilation**: `tsc --noEmit` clean after every commit across `mcp_server/` + `scripts/`\n- **Targeted vitest**: each commit's new/modified test file passed before the commit landed\n- **Broader regression**: Wave B merge-complete sweep `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run tests/code-graph tests/hook tests/handler-save` → 145/145 green\n\n### Final smoke gate (this commit)\n\n- `tsc --noEmit` across `mcp_server` and `scripts`: **PASSED (zero errors)**\n- Full Wave A + B + C + D test files: **PASSED** (49/49 Wave A, 98/98 hook regression, 145/145 code-graph+hook+handler-save, 117/117 Wave D targeted)\n- ~280 new/modified test cases across Phase 017 scope all passing\n\n### Known pre-existing failure (NOT a Phase 017 regression)\n\n`handler-memory-save.vitest.ts:3174` — `TypeError: parsed.qualityFlags is not iterable` at `memory-save.ts:368`. This bug originated in commit `104f534bd` (Phase 016 v3.4.0.1 P0-B composite) and is orthogonal to Phase 017 scope. Phase 018 or a follow-up should initialize `parsed.qualityFlags = []` defensively before the `Array.from(new Set([...])` call.\n\n### Known-verified at implementation time\n\n- H-56-1 fix proven working in practice: `/memory:save` invocations during the session cascaded `lastUpdated` refresh across 38 folder metadata files (commit `8859da9cd`)\n- H-56-2 cross-handler observability attack refuted by segment-2 iter 56: no in-tree caller composes `handleCodeGraphContext` + `handleCodeGraphQuery` results\n- H-56-3 partial-resolution via T-W1-HST-02 Docker deployment note (R53-P1w-001 hook-state surface only; SQLite CAS surface confirmed not reachable per segment-2 iter 56)\n- H-56-4 resolved via Cluster E (T-W1-HOK-01/02)\n- H-56-5 resolved via T-CNS-02 research backfill (`88063287b`)\n<!-- /ANCHOR:verification -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:limitations -->\n## Limitations\n\n### Deferred to Phase 019\n\n- **R55-P2-002**: Underused `importance-tier` helper at `importance-tiers.ts:149` (10+ inline duplicates)\n- **R55-P2-003**: `executeConflict` precondition-block DRY opportunity (tangential to T-RCB-DUP-01 which handled the big-block extraction)\n- **R55-P2-004**: YAML boolean-predicate evolution gap — `scalarsEqual` TRUE/FALSE coercion reachability under future YAML patterns\n\n### Known gaps\n\n- **description.json rich-content preservation**: The H-56-1 fix triggers `generate-description.js` auto-regen which overwrites hand-authored rich content (observed on 017 folder — lost cluster breakdowns + wave structure + blocking-findings block). Follow-up needed to either preserve existing non-autogen fields or opt rich-content folders out of regen.\n- **Copilot autonomous execution**: Cluster E landed but autonomous execution window (Phase 018+) hasn't been exercised end-to-end under cli-copilot-primary dispatch. Smoke-gate passed; production-load verification remains pending.\n- **T-SRS-BND-01 canary rollout**: Default is strict mode. `MCP_SESSION_RESUME_AUTH_MODE=permissive` flag enables canary but hasn't been canary-verified yet. Recommend verifying on a test MCP session before flipping default elsewhere.\n- **Pre-existing `qualityFlags` bug**: documented above, unrelated to Phase 017.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:limitations -->\n\n---\n\n## Sub-phase summaries\n\n### 001-initial-research\n\n**Status:** COMPLETE. Phase 016 remediation shipped 27 commits to `main` (2026-04-16–2026-04-17), then Phase 017 segment-2 deep-research (iterations 51-57) extended scope with 14 new findings. Total: 63 distinct findings closed (R1-001 through R50-002) + 4 P0 composites + 6 architectural primitives + 97 canonical tasks + 34 test tasks + 9 preflight tasks.\n\n**Six architectural primitives:** (1) `OperationResult<T>` uniform result shape — replaces booleans/nulls across 7 surfaces. (2) Zod `HookStateSchema` + `.bad` quarantine — schema validation on every `loadState()` call, closes P0-A cross-runtime poisoning. (3) Predecessor CAS in `executeConflict()` — SQL-level check-and-set blocks lineage forks at write boundary. (4) Batched snapshot reads in `reconsolidation-bridge.ts` — consistent scope decisions within one reconsolidation cycle. (5) mtime re-read after `readFileSync()` — prevents torn-read race in HookState. (6) `migrated: boolean` marker + ranking penalty flip — neutralizes graph-metadata laundering pipeline (P0-C).\n\n**H-56-1 confirmed (segment-2):** Default `/memory:save` was structural metadata-freshness no-op: `workflow.ts:1259 const ctxFileWritten = false` dead code + `workflow.ts:1333 plannerMode === 'full-auto'` gate with default `'plan-only'`. Fix shipped in `002-infrastructure-primitives`.\n\n**Notable:** `research/016-foundational-runtime-pt-01/` holds 50-iteration synthesis (`FINAL-synthesis-and-review.md`), findings registry, and closing-pass notes. `preflight-oq-resolution.md` resolves OQ1–OQ4.\n\n---\n\n### 002-infrastructure-primitives\n\n**Status:** COMPLETE. Wave A — 5 commits on `main` (2026-04-17). ~1.5h wall-clock via 3 concurrent Opus 4.7 subagents.\n\n**T-CNS-01 + T-W1-CNS-04 (H-56-1 fix, commit `aaf0f49a8`):** `workflow.ts:1259` — `const ctxFileWritten = false` replaced with `true`. `workflow.ts:1333` — `shouldRunExplicitSaveFollowUps = options.plannerMode === 'full-auto'` changed to `true` (unconditional). 38-folder `lastUpdated` cascade proven as side-effect. Test: `workflow-canonical-save-metadata.vitest.ts` (4/4 + 1 skipped).\n\n**T-CGC-01 (commit `4a154c555`):** `readiness-contract.ts` extracted from `handlers/code-graph/query.ts:225-300` — 4 helpers: `canonicalReadinessFromFreshness`, `queryTrustStateFromFreshness`, `buildQueryGraphMetadata`, `buildReadinessBlock`. 15 assertion tests.\n\n**T-W1-HOK-02 (commit `77da3013a`):** `hooks/shared-provenance.ts` extracted from `hooks/claude/shared.ts:100-140` — 3 functions including `escapeProvenanceField`, `sanitizeRecoveredPayload`, `wrapRecoveredCompactPayload`. Wired into `validate.sh --strict`. 22 tests.\n\n**T-CPN-01 (commit `0c9d6f612`):** CP-002 in `016/001-initial-research/implementation-summary.md` correctly amended to RESOLVED (by `e774eef07` T-PIN-08); original mislabeling corrected.\n\n**T-W1-MCX-01 (commit `ad02986fe`):** `memory-context.ts:200+425` `readiness` field renamed to `advisoryPreset` (was always-literal `'ready'`). Wave D — 3 commits on `main` (2026-04-17).\n\n**D1 typing hardening (commit `787bf4f88`):** `mcp_server/lib/utils/exhaustiveness.ts` with `assertNever` wired across 8 unions. Post-insert lookup uses `satisfies Record<OnIndexSkipReason, EnrichmentSkipReason>`.\n\n**D2 extraction refactors (commit `0ac9cdcba`):** `runEnrichmentStep` extracted; `runPostInsertEnrichment` reduced to short coordinator. Reconsolidation conflict path reuses one atomic transaction helper.\n\n**D3 YAML and deployment docs (commit `b26514cbc`):** `spec_kit_complete_confirm.yaml` `post_save_indexing` moved from prose `when:` to canonical `after:`. `DEPLOYMENT.md` published with `-v /tmp:/tmp` Docker anti-pattern warning, Copilot concurrency limits, AsyncLocalStorage caller context, and strict-by-default session-resume auth guidance.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3285:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/001-local-embeddings-foundation/016-llama-cpp-retrieval-quality-probe/scratch/probe-corpus.json:1063:    "content": "---\ntitle: \"Tasks: Memory Indexer Invariants [system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/005-memory-indexer-invariants/tasks]\"\ndescription: \"Unified task ledger for both invariant tracks: PE lineage guard + fromScan recheck bypass (Track A), shared index-scope SSOT + multi-layer constitutional-tier enforcement + transactional cleanup CLI (Track B), plus Wave-1 SQL-layer remediation, Wave-2 hardening, and the phase-merge restructure.\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"026/010 tasks\"\n  - \"memory indexer invariants tasks\"\n  - \"index scope invariants tasks\"\n  - \"constitutional tier cleanup tasks\"\nimportance_tier: \"important\"\ncontextType: \"implementation\"\ntemplate_source_hint: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: tasks-core + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\"\n_memory:\n  continuity:\n    packet_pointer: \"system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/005-memory-indexer-invariants\"\n    last_updated_at: \"2026-04-24T19:25:00+02:00\"\n    last_updated_by: \"claude-opus-4-7\"\n    recent_action: \"Merged Track A and Track B tasks into root ledger\"\n    next_safe_action: \"Restart MCP and rerun memory_index_scan on 026/009 to close Track A live acceptance\"\n    blockers:\n      - \"Track A live MCP rescan on 026/009 still pending\"\n    completion_pct: 95\n    status: \"code-complete-pending-track-a-live-rescan\"\n    open_questions: []\n    answered_questions: []\ntemplate_source_marker: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: tasks-core + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\"\n---\n# Task Breakdown: Memory Indexer Invariants\n\n<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 3 -->\n<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: tasks-core + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:notation -->\n## Task Notation\n\n| Prefix | Meaning |\n|--------|---------|\n| `[ ]` | Pending |\n| `[x]` | Completed with evidence in `checklist.md` or `implementation-summary.md` |\n| `[P]` | Parallelizable |\n| `[B]` | Blocked |\n\n**Task Format**: `T### [P?] Description (file path)`\n\n### AI Execution Protocol\n\n**Pre-Task Checklist**\n- Re-read each runtime file immediately before patching it.\n- Keep edits inside this packet, `mcp_server/`, `scripts/memory/`, and the 026 parent metadata.\n- Run packet validation, focused tests, and cleanup verify before marking a task complete.\n\n**Execution Rules**\n\n| Rule ID | Rule | Why |\n|---------|------|-----|\n| TASK-SEQ-A | Track A Fix A lands before Fix B (canonical path plumbing → A2 guard → fromScan wiring). | A2 depends on `canonical_file_path` being available in `SimilarMemory`. |\n| TASK-SEQ-B | Track B shared helper lands before scanner and save-path callers. | Prevents policy drift from reappearing during the patch sequence. |\n| TASK-SCOPE | Track A and Track B edits share `memory-save.ts`; re-read before each patch. | Avoid accidentally reverting either track while editing the shared file. |\n| TASK-DB | Every cleanup mutation runs inside one SQLite transaction; `--apply` rebuilds its plan inside the transaction. | Prevents partial cleanup states and TOCTOU gaps. |\n| TASK-VERIFY | Record exact exit codes and before/after counts in `implementation-summary.md` and `checklist.md`. | Keeps verification auditable. |\n\n**Status Reporting Format**\n- Start state: which track and runtime surface is being changed.\n- Work state: which invariant, remediation wave, or cleanup pass is active.\n- End state: validation / tests / cleanup outcome — passing, pending, or blocked.\n\n**Blocked Task Protocol**\n- Mark a task `[B]` if a schema relationship or DB reference cannot be updated safely.\n- Stop the cleanup apply path on the first SQL error and preserve rollback.\n- Record blockers in `implementation-summary.md` before continuing elsewhere.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:notation -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:phase-1 -->\n## Phase 1: Setup\n\n### Investigation and Baseline\n\n- [x] **T-01** Read `/tmp/codex-lineage-investigation-output.txt` and confirm Track A root causes. [EVIDENCE: investigation summary matches the PE lineage path and the scan-recheck race.]\n- [x] **T-02** Read the live code paths before editing: PE gating, prediction-error decision logic, scan batching, reconsolidation bridge, save-time transaction flow, memory discovery, spec-doc classification, code-graph walkers. [EVIDENCE: `pe-gating.ts`, `prediction-error-gate.ts`, `memory-index.ts`, `reconsolidation-bridge.ts`, `memory-save.ts`, `memory-index-discovery.ts`, `spec-doc-paths.ts`, `structural-indexer.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-03** Live-inspect the Voyage-4 DB for Track B baseline counts. [EVIDENCE: 5700 constitutional / 2 in-folder / 5947 z_future / 0 external / duplicate gate-enforcement rule file at ids 1 + 9868.]\n- [x] **T-04** Create shared index-scope helper for memory and code graph. [EVIDENCE: `mcp_server/lib/utils/index-scope.ts` with `shouldIndexForMemory`, `shouldIndexForCodeGraph`, `EXCLUDED_FOR_MEMORY`.]\n- [x] **T-05** Create realpath canonicalization helper. [EVIDENCE: `mcp_server/lib/utils/canonical-path.ts` wraps `fs.realpathSync` with fail-open fallback.]\n- [x] **T-06** Centralize governance-audit action strings and helpers. [EVIDENCE: `mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts` exports `GOVERNANCE_AUDIT_ACTIONS`, `recordTierDowngradeAudit()`, `buildGovernanceLogicalKey()`.]\n<!-- /ANCHOR:phase-1 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:phase-2 -->\n## Phase 2: Implementation\n\n### Track A — Lineage and Concurrency\n\n- [x] **T-10** Add canonical path plumbing to `SimilarMemory` so PE orchestration can reason about same-file identity. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/save/types.ts`, `handlers/pe-gating.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-11** Implement Fix A with A2: downgrade cross-file `UPDATE` and `REINFORCE` decisions to `CREATE` in PE orchestration. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/save/pe-orchestration.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-12** Roll back Fix B1 by removing the forced `scanBatchSize = 1` override. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-index.ts` uses `BATCH_SIZE`.]\n- [x] **T-13** Implement Fix B2: mark scan-originated saves with `fromScan: true` and skip the transactional reconsolidation recheck only for those saves. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-index.ts`, `handlers/memory-save.ts`.]\n\n### Track B — Baseline Invariants\n\n- [x] **T-20** Wire memory discovery and spec-doc classification to the shared helper. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-index-discovery.ts`, `lib/config/spec-doc-paths.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-21** Align `isMemoryFile()` with the rule-file-only constitutional policy. [EVIDENCE: `lib/parsing/memory-parser.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-22** Add save-time path rejection and constitutional-tier downgrade. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-save.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-23** [P] Wire code-graph recursive and specific-file scanning to the shared helper while preserving existing excludes. [EVIDENCE: `code-graph/lib/structural-indexer.ts`, `code-graph/lib/indexer-types.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-24** [P] Add cleanup CLI with dry-run, apply, and verify modes. [EVIDENCE: `scripts/memory/cleanup-index-scope-violations.ts`.]\n\n### Track B — Wave-1 Storage-Layer Remediation\n\n- [x] **T-30** Hoist the constitutional-tier guard into SQL-layer update writes. [EVIDENCE: `lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-31** Protect post-insert metadata tier writes. [EVIDENCE: `lib/storage/post-insert-metadata.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-32** Re-assert index-scope and constitutional-tier invariants during checkpoint restore inside the barrier-held transaction. [EVIDENCE: `lib/storage/checkpoints.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-33** Emit durable `governance_audit` rows for non-constitutional tier downgrade attempts without failing the write path. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-save.ts`, `lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts`, `lib/storage/checkpoints.ts` emit `action='tier_downgrade_non_constitutional_path'`.]\n\n### Track B — Wave-2 Hardening\n\n- [x] **T-40** Close the cleanup-script audit gap: retain historical `governance_audit` rows for deleted memories; emit `tier_downgrade_non_constitutional_path_cleanup` per downgraded row. [EVIDENCE: `scripts/memory/cleanup-index-scope-violations.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-41** Collapse spec-doc exclusion drift onto `index-scope.ts` as SSOT; prove the helper drives classification + discovery. [EVIDENCE: `lib/config/spec-doc-paths.ts`, `handlers/memory-index-discovery.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-42** Harden save-time and code-graph `specificFiles` checks with `fs.realpathSync` canonicalization. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-save.ts`, `code-graph/lib/structural-indexer.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-43** Move cleanup apply planning onto the transaction snapshot; add walker DoS caps for `.gitignore` size, depth, and node count. [EVIDENCE: `scripts/memory/cleanup-index-scope-violations.ts`, `handlers/memory-index-discovery.ts`, `code-graph/lib/structural-indexer.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-44** Extend `constitutional → critical` update auditing and refresh operator + spec docs. [EVIDENCE: `handlers/memory-crud-update.ts`, `api/index.ts`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md`.]\n<!-- /ANCHOR:phase-2 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:phase-3 -->\n## Phase 3: Verification\n\n### Focused Test Coverage\n\n- [x] **T-50** Add Fix A regression for `tasks.md` vs sibling checklist. [EVIDENCE: `tests/pe-orchestration.vitest.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-51** Replace the old B1 scan serialization test with B2 coverage for `fromScan` propagation and the non-scan control path. [EVIDENCE: `tests/handler-memory-index.vitest.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-52** [P] Add focused exclusion and constitutional-tier Vitest coverage. [EVIDENCE: `tests/index-scope.vitest.ts`, `tests/memory-save-index-scope.vitest.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-53** [P] Add SQL-layer update + checkpoint-restore invariant coverage. [EVIDENCE: `tests/memory-crud-update-constitutional-guard.vitest.ts`, `tests/checkpoint-restore-invariant-enforcement.vitest.ts`.]\n- [x] **T-54** [P] Add Wave-2 coverage for cleanup audits, SSOT unification, realpath, walker DoS caps, and governance helpers. [EVIDENCE: `tests/cleanup-script-audit-emission.vitest.ts`, `tests/exclusion-ssot-unification.vitest.ts`, `tests/symlink-realpath-hardening.vitest.ts`, `stress_test/code-graph/walker-dos-caps.vitest.ts`, `tests/memory-governance.vitest.ts`.]\n\n### Build and Suite\n\n- [x] **T-60** Run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` for `mcp_server` and `scripts`. [EVIDENCE: all four exit `0`.]\n- [x] **T-61** Run the Track A focused regression pair. [EVIDENCE: `26/26` passed.]\n- [x] **T-62** Run the Wave-2 focused Vitest set. [EVIDENCE: `20/20` passed across 8 files.]\n- [x] **T-63** Run the README-regression set. [EVIDENCE: `218/218` passed across 4 files.]\n- [x] **T-64** Run `timeout 300 npm run test:core` and record carryover failures honestly. [EVIDENCE: exit `124`; unrelated failures in `tests/copilot-hook-wiring.vitest.ts` and `tests/stage3-rerank-regression.vitest.ts` reproduced in isolation.]\n- [x] **T-65** Run packet strict validation. [EVIDENCE: `validate.sh --strict --no-recursive` exit `0`.]\n\n### Live Cleanup\n\n- [x] **T-70** Run cleanup dry-run / apply / verify against the Voyage-4 DB. [EVIDENCE: `--apply` deleted 5945 vector rows, 254 memory rows, 252 history rows, 251 lineage rows, 9 feedback rows, 10811 other reference rows; rewrote 2 feedback + 3 lineage refs; `--verify` exit `0` with final counts `constitutional_total=2`, `constitutional_in_folder=2`, `z_future_rows=0`, `external_rows=0`, `invalid_constitutional_rows=0`.]\n- [x] **T-71** Update operator README with invariants, helper location, and stable governance-audit action strings. [EVIDENCE: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md` \"Index Scope Invariants\" section.]\n\n### Root Merge Restructure\n\n- [x] **T-80** Merge Track A and Track B phase docs into root `spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, `decision-record.md`, `implementation-summary.md`. [EVIDENCE: root docs rewritten with consolidated context; no copy/paste.]\n- [x] **T-81** Update root `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json` to reflect merged state (no children). [EVIDENCE: `children_ids=[]`, merged `key_files`, `trigger_phrases`, and `entities`.]\n- [x] **T-82** Remove `001-memory-indexer-lineage-and-concurrency-fix/` and `002-index-scope-and-constitutional-tier-invariants/` phase folders. [EVIDENCE: `git rm -r`.]\n- [x] **T-83** Add legacy-path aliases to `graph-metadata.json.aliases` so external cross-references keep resolving. [EVIDENCE: aliases array contains the two flat pre-move paths and the two phase-path forms.]\n\n### Pending Live Acceptance\n\n- [ ] **T-90** Restart MCP, rerun `memory_index_scan` on `026/007-hook-parity`, and confirm zero `E_LINEAGE` plus zero `candidate_changed`. (BLOCKED: requires user restart + live embedding-capable runtime.)\n<!-- /ANCHOR:phase-3 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:completion -->\n## Completion Criteria\n\n- [x] All Track A code changes landed with regression coverage\n- [x] All Track B invariants enforced at every mutation surface (discovery, save, SQL update, post-insert, checkpoint restore, code-graph scan)\n- [x] Wave-1 + Wave-2 remediations merged with focused tests\n- [x] Cleanup CLI dry-run / apply / verify exited `0` against the live DB\n- [x] Operator README + spec docs reflect the final invariant set\n- [x] Phase folders merged into root docs\n- [ ] Track A live MCP rescan on `026/007-hook-parity` — pending user restart + embedding-capable runtime\n<!-- /ANCHOR:completion -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:cross-refs -->\n## Cross-References\n\n- **Specification**: `spec.md`\n- **Plan**: `plan.md`\n- **Checklist**: `checklist.md`\n- **Decisions**: `decision-record.md`\n- **Implementation Summary**: `implementation-summary.md`\n- **Investigation Source**: `/tmp/codex-lineage-investigation-output.txt`\n<!-- /ANCHOR:cross-refs -->",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3286:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/003-embedder-testing-and-architecture/001-local-embeddings-foundation/016-llama-cpp-retrieval-quality-probe/scratch/probe-corpus.json:1095:    "content": "---\ntitle: \"...kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/005-opencode-plugin-loader-bridge-fixes/decision-record]\"\ndescription: \"ADRs for scoping decisions. ADR-001 captures the OpenCode 1.3.17 plugin discovery contract, ADR-002/003 record loader remediation, and ADR-004 records the skill-advisor hook remap from ignored Claude-style hooks to OpenCode event + experimental.chat.system.transform.\"\ntrigger_phrases:\n  - \"026/009/007 adr\"\n  - \"opencode plugin loader decisions\"\nimportance_tier: \"important\"\ncontextType: \"planning\"\ntemplate_source_hint: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: decision-record + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\"\n_memory:\n  continuity:\n    packet_pointer: \"system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-hook-parity/005-opencode-plugin-loader-bridge-fixes\"\n    last_updated_at: \"2026-04-28T19:30:00Z\"\n    last_updated_by: \"codex-gpt-5-hygiene-pass\"\n    recent_action: \"Hygiene pass - validator structure\"\n    next_safe_action: \"Keep validators green\"\n    completion_pct: 100\ntemplate_source_marker: \"<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: decision-record + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\"\n---\n# Decision Record: OpenCode Plugin Loader Remediation\n\n<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 3 -->\n<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: decision-record + level3-arch | v2.2 -->\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001 -->\n## ADR-001: OpenCode 1.3.17 plugin discovery contract — empirical baseline\n\n**Status**: Accepted (Phase 1 probe complete, 2026-04-22T15:12:00+02:00).\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-context -->\n### Context\n\nOpenCode 1.3.17 ships as a single bun-bundled binary. The TUI worker auto-discovers plugins from `.opencode/plugins/` at startup. Empirical observation (drafting session, 2026-04-22T13:30Z): the worker crashes with `TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating 'plugin2.auth')` when a discovered file resolves to `undefined` because it lacks a default export. The crash occurs at `/$bunfs/root/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.js:301126:25` — a bundled, non-source-readable location.\n\nThe contract details (glob shape, extension filter, opt-out mechanism, undefined-handling, recommended helper-module location) were confirmed from official docs, the installed package type contract, the installed 1.3.17 binary strings, and empirical TUI probes.\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-context -->\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-decision -->\n### Decision\n\nTreat `.opencode/plugins/` as a flat plugin-entrypoint directory. In OpenCode 1.3.17, the server-side local plugin scanner loads JavaScript/TypeScript files from `{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}` under each config directory. The official documentation says files placed in `.opencode/plugins/` and `~/.config/opencode/plugins/` are automatically loaded at startup and that a plugin module exports plugin functions. The installed `@opencode-ai/plugin` types define a `Plugin` as a function returning `Hooks`, while the 1.3.17 binary still supports a legacy path that treats every exported function in a loaded `.js` module as a plugin function. That legacy behavior is the immediate null-hook source for `spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`: its named `parseTransportPlan` export is invoked as a second plugin, returns `null` for a plugin input object, and later the auth loop crashes while reading `plugin2.auth`.\n\n**Discovery contract matrix**:\n\n| Aspect                            | Observed in OpenCode 1.3.17 | Evidence |\n|-----------------------------------|-----------------------------|----------|\n| Discovery glob                    | `{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}` from config directories | `strings /Users/michelkerkmeester/.opencode/bin/opencode | rg \"{plugin,plugins}\"`; newer local source snapshot has the same `Glob.scan(\"{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}\", { dot: true, symlink: true })` shape |\n| Recursive vs flat                 | Flat only                   | The glob has a single `*` segment, not `**/*`; helper subdirectories are outside the discovered entrypoint set |\n| Extension filter                  | `.ts` and `.js` only in the installed 1.3.17 binary | Binary string evidence; official docs say JavaScript/TypeScript files, and do not mention `.mjs` |\n| Default-export shape required     | V1 modules default-export an object with `server()` or `tui()`; legacy modules may export plugin functions directly | `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/index.d.ts`; binary strings for `must default export an object with server()` and `Plugin export is not a function` |\n| Behavior on missing default export | Missing default is not enough by itself if no exported functions exist, but any loaded exported function returning `null`/`undefined` leaves a null hook and crashes at auth iteration | Probe + log: TUI emitted `TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating 'plugin2.auth')`; log recorded project `.js` plugins loaded and `plugin config hook failed` with `hook.config` null |\n| Behavior on `export default null` | Equivalent risk if the loaded plugin resolves to `null` | Inferred from auth loop string `plugin2.auth` and the observed null-hook crash |\n| Behavior on `export default undefined` | Equivalent risk if the loaded plugin resolves to `undefined`/missing hook object | Inferred from legacy loader contract and observed null-hook crash |\n| Opt-out: filename prefix          | No documented opt-out found | Official docs list plugin directories and npm config; no filename-prefix exclusion documented |\n| Opt-out: opencode.json `plugins.exclude` | No documented opt-out found | Official config docs expose `plugin` package list, not local-folder exclude rules |\n| Opt-out: manifest file            | No documented manifest opt-out for local plugin folders | Installed package type contract and official docs do not define a local manifest exclusion |\n| Recommended helper-module location | Any sibling folder outside `{plugin,plugins}/` is safe; use `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` locally | Flat glob evidence plus the packet's Outcome A target layout |\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-decision -->\n### Methodology\n\n1. Checked official OpenCode plugin docs: local files in `.opencode/plugins/` and `~/.config/opencode/plugins/` are startup-loaded, and plugin modules export plugin functions.\n2. Inspected the installed dependency at `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/`; the local install is `@opencode-ai/plugin` package metadata `1.3.15` under a `1.3.17` OpenCode binary, but the exposed `Plugin`/`Hooks` contract is sufficient for the hook-object shape.\n3. Inspected the installed 1.3.17 binary with `strings` to recover the local glob and auth-loop crash site.\n4. Ran empirical probes with `XDG_STATE_HOME=/tmp/opencode-state XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/opencode-data XDG_CACHE_HOME=/tmp/opencode-cache` so the sandbox did not block OpenCode state writes. `opencode --version` returned `1.3.17`; `timeout 4 opencode` reproduced the `plugin2.auth` JSON envelope.\n5. Checked structured logs under `/tmp/opencode-data/opencode/log/`; they showed the two project `.js` plugins loaded, no `.mjs` helpers loaded, and a null `hook.config` failure before the TUI bootstrap error.\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-consequences -->\n### Consequences\n\n- **Positive**: ADR-002 can choose the remediation outcome with full confidence in the contract semantics.\n- **Positive**: The contract documentation becomes a reusable reference for any future OpenCode plugin authoring or upgrade probe.\n- **Negative**: Phase 1 must complete before Phase 2 can begin; partial-knowledge implementation risks shipping against assumption.\n\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001 -->\n\n---\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-consequences -->\n### ADR-002: Remediation outcome selection (A / B / C)\n\n**Status**: Accepted.\n\n### Context\n\nThree feasible remediation paths emerged during drafting. The choice depends on ADR-001's confirmation of the OpenCode plugin discovery contract.\n\n### Decision\n\nChoose **Outcome A: move helpers out of `.opencode/plugins/`**, with one compatibility correction inside the already in-scope compact plugin: make the parser helper safe when OpenCode 1.3.17's legacy loader invokes named function exports as plugins. This preserves the packet's primary file-organization outcome and addresses the empirically observed null-hook crash source.\n\n**Decision matrix**:\n\n| # | Outcome                                                                                | Mechanism                                                                                                                | Feasibility (depends on ADR-001) | Eng. cost | Runtime cost | Coverage / Risk |\n|---|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------|-----------|--------------|-----------------|\n| A | **Move helpers out of `.opencode/plugins/`**                                           | `git mv` 3 helper files to `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/`; update `BRIDGE_PATH` constants and schema-helper import; guard the compact plugin's named parser export against legacy-loader invocation | HIGH — discovery is flat and outside sibling folders | Low (~30-60 min) | Negligible | Chosen. Eliminates helper drift, documents entrypoints-only convention, and fixes the observed null hook. |\n| B | **Add no-op default exports to the 3 helper files**                                    | Append `export default async function() { return {}; }` to each helper                                                   | LOW benefit — installed 1.3.17 does not load `.mjs`; would not address the named parser export | Lowest (~10 min) | Marginal — zombie plugins if extensions change later | Rejected. Masks folder impurity and does not match the empirical crash source. |\n| C | **Use OpenCode opt-out mechanism (filename prefix or opencode.json exclude)**          | Rename helpers to `_<name>.mjs` or add `plugins.exclude: [\"*-bridge.mjs\", \"*-schema.mjs\"]` to opencode.json              | INFEASIBLE — no documented local-folder opt-out surfaced | Low (~30 min) | Negligible | Rejected. Depends on unsupported behavior. |\n\n**Selected primary path**: A (full isolation plus legacy export hardening). It does not depend on undocumented OpenCode behavior, has a clear file-system convention, and handles the observed 1.3.17 legacy loader behavior.\n\n**Fallback if A is infeasible** (e.g., discovery is recursive into `.opencode/`): B is the safest universal fallback.\n\n**Use C only if** ADR-001 surfaces a stable, documented opt-out — otherwise its dependency on undocumented behavior makes it brittle across OpenCode upgrades.\n\n### Consequences (Outcome A)\n\n- **Positive**: TUI starts cleanly. Plugin folder becomes self-documenting (every file is an entrypoint).\n- **Positive**: Future contributors cannot accidentally re-introduce the crash by adding a helper next to a plugin (regression test catches it).\n- **Positive**: Bridge subprocess architecture preserved — bridges still run as spawned `node` processes, just from a different parent path.\n- **Positive**: The compact plugin's test-only parser helper no longer creates a null hook if a legacy loader treats it as a plugin function.\n- **Negative**: One extra folder (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/`) to know about. Mitigated by README convention doc.\n- **Negative**: Move breaks existing bridge path references — T-10 inventory governs which references are updated.\n\n### References\n\n- Reproduction evidence: drafting session 2026-04-22T13:30Z reproduced the crash in both Public root and Barter.\n- Official docs: https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/ and https://opencode.ai/docs/config/\n- Installed package: `.opencode/node_modules/@opencode-ai/plugin/` (inspected during Phase 1).\n- Installed binary: `/Users/michelkerkmeester/.opencode/bin/opencode` and wrapper `/Users/michelkerkmeester/.superset/bin/opencode`.\n- Bundled worker: `/$bunfs/root/src/cli/cmd/tui/worker.js` (non-source-readable; behavior inferred from crash signature, binary strings, logs, and empirical probe).\n- Sibling phases: `../002-copilot-custom-instructions-hook-parity/`, `../003-codex-native-startup-advisor-hooks/` (different runtime, different gap).\n\n---\n\n<!-- ANCHOR:adr-001-impl -->\n### ADR-003: Implementation outcome — Outcome A shipped\n\n**Status**: Accepted (implemented 2026-04-22T15:21Z).\n<!-- /ANCHOR:adr-001-impl -->\n### Context\n\nPhase 2 implemented the ADR-002 selection after the empirical contract investigation. The helper files were removed from `.opencode/plugins/`, which is now documented as an entrypoint-only folder.\n\nOne additional code-level compatibility correction was necessary inside the in-scope compact plugin. The installed 1.3.17 loader has a legacy path that can treat named function exports as plugin factories. `parseTransportPlan()` is exported for tests; when invoked with a plugin context object instead of a response string, it previously returned `null`, which could reproduce the same null-hook failure class.
