     3	import { z } from '../../../system-spec-kit/mcp_server/node_modules/zod/index.js';
     4	
     5	// ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
     6	
     7	export const EXECUTOR_KINDS = ['native', 'cli-codex', 'cli-gemini', 'cli-claude-code', 'cli-opencode', 'cli-devin'] as const;
     8	export type ExecutorKind = typeof EXECUTOR_KINDS[number];
     9	
    10	export const REASONING_EFFORTS = ['none', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh'] as const;
    11	export type ReasoningEffort = typeof REASONING_EFFORTS[number];
    12	
    13	export const SERVICE_TIERS = ['priority', 'standard', 'fast'] as const;
    14	export type ServiceTier = typeof SERVICE_TIERS[number];
    15	
    16	const SANDBOX_MODES = ['read-only', 'workspace-write', 'danger-full-access'] as const;
    17	export type SandboxMode = typeof SANDBOX_MODES[number];
    18	export type GeminiSandboxMode = 'docker' | 'none';
    19	export type ClaudePermissionMode = 'plan' | 'acceptEdits' | 'bypassPermissions';
    20	
    21	// ───── CONSTANTS ─────
    22	
    23	export const executorConfigSchema = z.object({
    24	  kind: z.enum(EXECUTOR_KINDS).default('native'),
    25	  model: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
    26	  reasoningEffort: z.enum(REASONING_EFFORTS).nullable().default(null),
    27	  serviceTier: z.enum(SERVICE_TIERS).nullable().default(null),
    28	  sandboxMode: z.enum(SANDBOX_MODES).nullable().default(null),
    29	  timeoutSeconds: z.number().int().positive().default(900),
    30	});
    31	
    32	export type ExecutorConfig = z.infer<typeof executorConfigSchema>;
    33	
    34	export const EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT: Record<ExecutorKind, readonly (keyof ExecutorConfig)[]> = {
    35	  native: [],
    36	  'cli-codex': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'serviceTier', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    37	  'cli-gemini': ['model', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    38	  'cli-claude-code': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    39	  'cli-opencode': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    40	  'cli-devin': ['model', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    41	};
    42	
    43	export const GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS = ['gemini-3.1-pro-preview'] as const;
    44	export type GeminiSupportedModel = typeof GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS[number];
    45	
    46	export const DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS = ['swe-1.6', 'deepseek-v4', 'glm-5.1', 'kimi-k2.6'] as const;
    47	export type DevinSupportedModel = typeof DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS[number];
    48	export type DevinPermissionMode = 'auto' | 'dangerous';
    49	
    50	// ───── DOMAIN ERRORS ─────
    51	
    52	type ExecutorConfigIssue = {
    53	  path: PropertyKey[];
    54	  message: string;
    55	};
    56	
    57	export class ExecutorNotWiredError extends Error {
    58	  kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-gemini' | 'cli-claude-code'>;
    59	
    60	  constructor(kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-gemini' | 'cli-claude-code'>) {
    61	    super(`Executor kind '${kind}' is reserved in the schema but not yet wired. Awaiting future spec for ${kind} integration.`);
    62	    this.name = 'ExecutorNotWiredError';
    63	    this.kind = kind;
    64	  }
    65	}
    66	
    67	export class ExecutorConfigError extends Error {
    68	  issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[];
    69	
    70	  constructor(input: { issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[] }) {
    71	    super(input.issues.map((issue) => `${issue.path.map(String).join('.') || '<root>'}: ${issue.message}`).join('; '));
    72	    this.name = 'ExecutorConfigError';
    73	    this.issues = input.issues;
    74	  }
    75	}
    76	
    77	// ───── HELPERS ─────
    78	
    79	function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
    80	  return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
    81	}
    82	
    83	function normalizeSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
    84	  return mode ?? 'workspace-write';
    85	}
    86	
    87	function normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw: unknown): unknown {
    88	  if (!isRecord(raw) || !Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(raw, 'type')) {
    89	    return raw;
    90	  }
    91	
    92	  const legacyKind = raw.type;
    93	  if (typeof legacyKind !== 'string') {
    94	    return raw;
    95	  }
    96	
    97	  if (typeof raw.kind === 'string' && raw.kind !== legacyKind) {
    98	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
    99	      issues: [{
   100	        path: ['type'],
   101	        message: `deprecated executor field 'type' conflicts with canonical kind '${raw.kind}'`,
   102	      }],
   103	    });
   104	  }
   105	
   106	  console.warn("[executor-config] Deprecated executor field 'type' was provided; use 'kind' instead.");
   107	  const { type: _legacyType, ...rest } = raw;
   108	  return {
   109	    ...rest,
   110	    kind: typeof raw.kind === 'string' ? raw.kind : legacyKind,
   111	  };
   112	}
   113	
   114	function normalizeIssues(error: z.ZodError<ExecutorConfig>): ExecutorConfigIssue[] {
   115	  return error.issues.map((issue) => ({
   116	    path: issue.path,
   117	    message: issue.message,
   118	  }));
   119	}
   120	
   121	// ───── EXPORTS ─────
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Codex CLI sandbox mode.
   125	 *
   126	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   127	 * @returns Codex-compatible sandbox mode.
   128	 */
   129	export function resolveCodexSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
   130	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode);
   131	}
   132	
   133	/**
   134	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Gemini CLI sandbox mode.
   135	 *
   136	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   137	 * @returns 'docker' for workspace-write/read-only, 'none' for danger-full-access.
   138	 */
   139	export function resolveGeminiSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): GeminiSandboxMode {
   140	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode) === 'danger-full-access' ? 'none' : 'docker';
   141	}
   142	
   143	/**
   144	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Claude Code permission mode.
   145	 *
   146	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   147	 * @returns Claude-compatible permission mode.
   148	 */
   149	export function resolveClaudePermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): ClaudePermissionMode {
   150	  switch (normalizeSandboxMode(mode)) {
   151	    case 'read-only':
   152	      return 'plan';
   153	    case 'danger-full-access':
   154	      return 'bypassPermissions';
   155	    default:
   156	      return 'acceptEdits';
   157	  }
   158	}
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Devin CLI permission mode.
   162	 *
   163	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   164	 * @returns 'auto' for workspace-write/read-only, 'dangerous' for danger-full-access.
   165	 */
   166	export function resolveDevinPermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): DevinPermissionMode {
   167	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode) === 'danger-full-access' ? 'dangerous' : 'auto';
   168	}
   169	
   170	/**
   171	 * Parse and validate a raw executor configuration.
   172	 *
   173	 * Normalizes legacy `type` fields, validates against the Zod schema,
   174	 * and enforces executor-kind-specific field support.
   175	 *

   177	 * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.

   179	 */

   192	    });
   193	  }

   197	    });

     1	---
     2	title: "Phase 007: Native per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out"
     3	description: "Design (spec only) a first-class contract-compliant per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out: an operator requests a fixed-count heterogeneous schedule (e.g. nine iterations on minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed then one on claude-opus-4-8) via /deep:start-review-loop, /deep:start-research-loop, and shared deep-loop-runtime, so future runs never hand-roll a worker pool like packet 122 review did. Models the schedule as ordered single-executor lineages (bands) over existing fan-out via a thin iterationPlan flag desugaring one-to-one into ordered lineages plus minimal sequencing. Produces schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, command-surface design; implementation is this phase's future work."
     4	trigger_phrases:
     5	  - "123 phase 007 model schedule"
     6	  - "per-iteration model schedule fan-out"
     7	importance_tier: "important"
     8	contextType: "implementation"
     9	_memory:

    13	    last_updated_by: "claude-opus"

    20	---
    21	<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: spec-core | v2.2 -->
    22	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 2 -->
    23	
    24	# Phase 007 — Native per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out
    25	
    26	## Purpose
    27	Design (spec only) a first-class, contract-compliant way for an operator to request a fixed-count heterogeneous per-iteration model schedule through the canonical `/deep:start-review-loop` and `/deep:start-research-loop` commands and the shared `deep-loop-runtime`. Motivating example: nine iterations on `minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` then one on `claude-opus-4-8`, so future runs never hand-roll a worker pool the way packet 122 review did. This phase produces the schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, and command-surface design; implementation is this phase's own future work (no code here).
    28	
    29	**Background — the packet 122 deviation:** packet 122 review ran a heterogeneous per-iteration model mix by hand-rolling a shell worker pool (`run_one.sh` with a `case` statement assigning per-iteration dimensions and a `xargs`-style fan-out). That bespoke machinery bypassed the shared `deep-loop-runtime` dispatch, the externalized JSONL state machine, and the consumer merge; it worked once but is unreproducible through the supported command surface and violates deep-review forbidden patterns.
    30	
    31	**Problem statement — the contract gap:** there is no supported way to express "run band A on model X for N iterations, then band B on model Y for M iterations" through the canonical commands. Existing fan-out (phases 001-006) runs homogeneous lineages concurrently and merges once all complete; it has no ordered heterogeneous per-iteration schedule and no ordered/seeded band sequencing, forcing operators back toward the packet 122 hand-rolled path.
    32	
    33	## Scope
    34	**In:**
    35	- Design a fixed-count heterogeneous per-iteration model-schedule capability expressible via the canonical `/deep:start-review-loop` and `/deep:start-research-loop` commands and the shared `deep-loop-runtime`.
    36	- Schema/flag design: an `iterationPlan` of ordered bands (from-to or count; executor kind and model; optional focus/dimensions; optional dependsOn/order) plus the `iteration-plan` (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) flag desugaring one-to-one into the existing `executors` lineage array, integrated through `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts` (reuse-first; no parallel dispatcher).
    37	- Ordered/sequential bands: a minimal `dependsOn`/`order` extension so a later band runs only after an earlier completes, with optional seed-context handoff, enforced inside the existing pool driver (no ad-hoc shell).
    38	- Per-band focus/dimension assignment (generalizing the packet 122 `run_one.sh` dimensions) threaded into existing per-iteration prompt construction for both review and research.
    39	- Per-iteration/per-band executor audit and tracing (band and model attribution) in the JSONL state log, deltas, and findings registry; plus merge-precedence as an ordered band-priority list in `fanout-merge.cjs` preserving review severity rollup and research dedup/attribution.
    40	- Contract-compliance and backward-compat design: satisfy every deep-review forbidden pattern (no ad-hoc shell fan-out; no direct CLI loop; no `/tmp` prompt dispatch; no Task-tool agent dispatch; no skipping the JSONL state machine; state under the local review/research packet); preserve read-only, externalized-state, and per-iteration-JSONL contracts; keep single-executor and homogeneous fan-out byte-identical with opt-in absent-by-default fields.
    41	- A Level-2 lean phase spec plus ordered tasks and risks numbered `007`, reusing the packet deep-research-before-build convention if siblings use it.
    42	
    43	**Out:**
    44	- Implementing the capability — design/spec only; the build is this phase's future work.
    45	- Any code edits to fan-out scripts, `executor-config.ts`, the auto/confirm YAML, or the deep-review / deep-research / deep-loop-runtime SKILL.md files in this phase.
    46	- Inventing a new parallel orchestration system, worker-pool runtime, or state machine that competes with `step_fanout_spawn` and `fanout-pool.cjs`.
    47	- True intra-lineage per-iteration executor rotation inside one continuous loop with cross-lineage iteration interleaving or band-synchronization barriers (rejected in favor of ordered homogeneous lineages unless desugaring proves insufficient).
    48	- Convergence-algorithm changes (newInfoRatio, Bayesian scoring) beyond making per-band max-iterations and ordered bands interoperate with existing convergence.
    49	- New executor kinds/providers/models beyond those registered (minimax, codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin, native) and changes to the single-executor default path or existing homogeneous fan-out merge semantics.
    50	- Re-litigating or modifying packet 122 hand-rolled artifacts.
    51	
    52	## Success
    53	- Command surface and one-to-one desugaring into existing fan-out lineages specified; desugaring pinned to real fan-out and `executor-config` entry points.
    54	- No parallel dispatcher introduced; existing machinery reused.
    55	- Ordered bands, seed-handoff, per-band focus, and model attribution covered.
    56	- Merge precedence keeps the severity-rollup invariant (any active P0 from any band forces merged FAIL).
    57	- Every deep-review forbidden pattern mapped and opt-in byte-identical proven.
    58	- Valid Level-2 packet 123 child numbered `007`.
    59	
    60	## Risks
    61	- **Sequencing gap:** existing fan-out runs lineages concurrently and jumps to synthesis once all complete; band B after band A with optional seeding needs an ordered/`dependsOn` extension to `fanout-pool.cjs` that does not yet exist, so it may be underestimated as pure sugar. Mitigation: front-load research to confirm whether ordering is sugar over concurrency caps or a genuine minimal pool-driver delta, and scope it explicitly.
    62	- **Reuse-vs-rewrite drift:** pressure to match packet 122 per-iteration-dimension UX could tempt true intra-loop executor rotation (the hard path needing band-sync barriers and iteration interleaving). Mitigation: hold the ordered-homogeneous-lineage framing and keep intra-loop rotation out of scope unless desugaring is proven insufficient.
    63	- **Merge-precedence regression:** generalizing the any-P0-to-FAIL rule into ordered band-priority risks weakening strongest-restriction, since a lower-priority band P0 must still escalate. Mitigation: specify precedence as conflict-resolution only, with severity rollup preserved as an invariant above band priority.
    64	- **Contract-compliance illusion and two-consumer divergence:** a loosely specified band-priority or ordered layer could resemble the forbidden ad-hoc dispatcher, and research dedup/attribution versus review severity-rollup may fit one design unevenly. Mitigation: explicit per-forbidden-pattern mapping; require all dispatch inside `step_fanout_spawn` and the fan-out scripts; specify the band-priority and audit model for both consumers with differences called out.
    65	- **API-assumption and scope-creep risk:** candidate files are enumerated but not re-read in this design pass, so specifying against a stale interface or drifting into code edits would mislead implementers. Mitigation: success criteria require locking the desugaring contract against actual entry points via the front-loaded research task; scope-out forbids code edits in this phase.
    66	
    67	## Out of scope
    68	Implementation/build of the capability; any code edits; a competing parallel orchestrator; true intra-lineage executor rotation; convergence-math changes; new executor kinds; packet 122 artifact changes (all deferred or excluded per Scope/Out above).

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Worker Pool                                   ║
     3	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     4	// ║ Concurrency-capped fan-out primitive for the opt-in multi-executor        ║
     5	// ║ ("fan-out") layer above the single-executor deep-loop. Generalizes the    ║
     6	// ║ council parallel dispatcher (lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs) by      ║
     7	// ║ adding a concurrency cap so N executor lineages run with at most K in      ║
     8	// ║ flight, plus a status-ledger writer mirroring the proven packet-122       ║
     9	// ║ orchestration-status.log.                                                  ║
    10	// ║                                                                           ║
    11	// ║ Design: pure pool primitive (worker is INJECTED) + ledger helpers, fully  ║
    12	// ║ unit-tested. The real spawn worker and the CLI entry that wires it        ║
    13	// ║ (arg parse, JSON-out, exit codes) live in fanout-run.cjs.                ║
    14	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    15	
    16	'use strict';
    17	
    18	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    19	// 1. IMPORTS
    20	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21	
    22	const fs = require('node:fs');
    23	const path = require('node:path');
    24	
    25	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	// 2. HELPERS
    27	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	
    29	function isRecord(value) {
    30	  return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
    31	}
    32	
    33	function normalizeTimestamp(value) {
    34	  if (value instanceof Date) {
    35	    return value.toISOString();
    36	  }
    37	  if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
    38	    return value;
    39	  }
    40	  return new Date().toISOString();
    41	}
    42	
    43	function normalizeConcurrency(value) {
    44	  const n = Number(value);
    45	  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < 1) {
    46	    return 1;
    47	  }
    48	  return Math.floor(n);
    49	}
    50	
    51	function labelFor(item, index) {
    52	  if (isRecord(item) && typeof item.label === 'string' && item.label.trim() !== '') {
    53	    return item.label;
    54	  }
    55	  return `item-${index}`;
    56	}
    57	
    58	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59	// 3. CORE LOGIC
    60	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * Run a single pool item through the worker and capture its result or error.
    64	 *
    65	 * Never throws: a worker rejection is captured as a `rejected` result so one
    66	 * failing lineage cannot sink the whole pool (mirrors council `settleSeat`).
    67	 *
    68	 * @param {Object} params - Settlement parameters.
    69	 * @param {*} params.item - The work item (e.g. a lineage descriptor).
    70	 * @param {number} params.index - Zero-based item index (preserves order).
    71	 * @param {Function} params.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
    72	 * @param {Function} params.now - Callable returning a Date or ISO timestamp.
    73	 * @param {Function} [params.onEvent] - Optional callback for ledger events.
    74	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result with label, status, timing, output|error.
    75	 */
    76	async function settleItem({ item, index, worker, now, onEvent }) {
    77	  const label = labelFor(item, index);
    78	  const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    79	  const startedAtMs = Date.now();
    80	
    81	  if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    82	    onEvent({ event: 'started', label, index, at: startedAtIso });
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  try {
    86	    const output = await worker(item, { index });
    87	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    88	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
    89	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    90	      onEvent({ event: 'completed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs });
    91	    }
    92	    return {
    93	      label,
    94	      status: 'fulfilled',
    95	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
    96	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
    97	      duration_ms: durationMs,
    98	      output,
    99	    };
   100	  } catch (error) {
   101	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   102	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
   103	    const normalizedError = {
   104	      name: error && error.name ? String(error.name) : 'Error',
   105	      message: error && error.message ? String(error.message) : String(error),
   106	    };
   107	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
   108	      onEvent({ event: 'failed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs, error: normalizedError });
   109	    }
   110	    return {
   111	      label,
   112	      status: 'rejected',
   113	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
   114	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   115	      duration_ms: durationMs,
   116	      error: normalizedError,
   117	    };
   118	  }
   119	}
   120	
   121	/**
   122	 * Run items through a worker with at most `concurrency` in flight.
   123	 *
   124	 * This is the fan-out generalization of council `dispatchCouncilSeats`: same
   125	 * never-throws per-item settlement and ordered results, plus a concurrency cap
   126	 * so a large lineage set runs K-at-a-time (the proven `xargs -P K` shape).
   127	 *
   128	 * @param {Object} options - Pool configuration.
   129	 * @param {Array} options.items - Work items (each ideally has a `label`).
   130	 * @param {number} options.concurrency - Max items in flight (clamped to >= 1).

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2703:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/z_archive/wave-4-2026-05-26-reorg/017-phase-reorg-and-renumber/scratch/rg/ref-013-embedder-testing-and-architecture-all.txt:1293:./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/013-embedder-testing-and-architecture/006-mcp-launcher-concurrency/002-cross-launcher-lease-propagation/review/prompts/iteration-001.md

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2754:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/011-deep-review-shutdown-and-codegraph/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:49:Covered the full close + WAL checkpoint lifecycle in vector-index-store.ts: close_db (1286-1311), checkpointAllWal (1313-1317), detachActiveVectorShard (804-814), attachActiveVectorShard ordering (749-798), the db_connections map (declared line 682, set at 1273, cleared at 1297), set_active_database_connection (693-729), and initialize_db close paths (1241/1254/1266). Verified barrel re-exports in vector-index.ts (lines 129-169) correctly surface close_db/checkpointAllWal/detachActiveVectorShard with no shadowing or missing exports. Confirmed no double-close of the active db (loop skips conn===db, then the if(db) block closes it) and no double-close on validation-failure paths (those connections are closed inline and never added to db_connections, which only happens after all validation passes at line 1273). Examined the checkpointAllWal/close_db TOCTOU on module-global `db`: NOT recorded as a finding because Node's single-threaded JS model with no await boundaries inside either function precludes real interleaving, and both pragmas are best-effort try/catch wrapped. COULD NOT verify at runtime: (1) whether better-sqlite3 DETACH actually leaves a non-empty shard -wal in practice (SQLite-version dependent) -- CORR-002 rests on the code's own documented TRUNCATE rationale, not a reproduced corruption; (2) actual call sites/concurrency of checkpointAllWal vs close_db from the daemon watchdog/signal handlers (the 007-mcp-daemon-reliability caller layer was out of scope and not read); (3) whether multi-path db_connections (>1 tracked connection) ever co-exist at runtime, which gates the severity of CORR-001/002/003 -- if only one connection is ever live, all three drop toward P2/non-issue.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2776:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/gpt55/iterations/iteration-001.md:107:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2812:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/024-daemon-concurrency-fixes/spec.md:64:Packet 046 deep research iteration-001 identified four concurrency defects in the skill-advisor daemon and freshness layer. Three are P1 (watcher flush reentrancy, lifecycle/shutdown ordering, generation lock-token theft) and one is P2 (out-of-order cache invalidation). Each defect lets the daemon's persisted state diverge from reality under realistic timer + shutdown + GC-pause interleavings.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4068:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/005-stress-test-expansion-alignment/research/prompts/iteration-001.md:77:For Uncovered + Partial features ranked by stress-test value (memory invariants, search quality regression, code-graph freshness, skill-advisor concurrency, matrix shadow comparison, session lifecycle), author new vitest files OR extend existing ones.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4191:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/003-continuity-refactor-gates/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:169:- **F1.2**: The `withSpecFolderLock` mutex (memory-save.ts:1569) is the critical concurrency primitive that MUST transfer to the new path. Without it, concurrent saves to the same anchor would race.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4219:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/002-spec-kit-internals/002-skill-advisor/001-skill-graph/002-skill-graph-daemon-native-advisor-tools/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:13:- `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/file-watcher.ts:299`-`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/file-watcher.ts:405` → Reindex scheduling resolves real paths, verifies containment, hashes content, skips unchanged files, bounds concurrency, retries `SQLITE_BUSY`, and records timing metrics.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4220:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/002-spec-kit-internals/002-skill-advisor/001-skill-graph/002-skill-graph-daemon-native-advisor-tools/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:45:The most important A1 design pressure is editor save behavior. Vim explicitly documents rename-and-new-file save modes that affect watcher daemons (`https://vimhelp.org/options.txt.html:L1064-L1101`), while Chokidar offers `atomic` and `awaitWriteFinish` knobs for atomic and chunked write patterns (`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/paulmillr/chokidar/main/README.md:L8-L19`). The repo's current generic helper already combines `awaitWriteFinish`, content hashing, containment checks, bounded concurrency, `SQLITE_BUSY` retry, delete cleanup, and event mapping (`file-watcher.ts:251-258`, `file-watcher.ts:299-405`, `file-watcher.ts:407-445`), and its tests cover delete, rename, burst rename, concurrent rename, debounce, timing, and retry cases (`file-watcher.vitest.ts:304-606`).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4302:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/003-memory-and-causal-runtime/001-continuity-memory-runtime/research/prompts/iteration-001.md:13:026 continuity-memory-runtime — correctness gaps, concurrency/race conditions, and doc-code drift across the parent packet (`.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/002-continuity-memory-runtime/`) and its four direct children:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:917:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/024-compact-code-graph/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:28:- P2-001: `session_resume` implements a `sessionId` override for cached-summary selection, but the public tool contract strips that field before callers can use it. The handler accepts `sessionId` and forwards it into cached-summary resolution [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts:87] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-resume.ts:409], while both schema layers only allow `specFolder` and `minimal` [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:669] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/schemas/tool-input-schemas.ts:442]. In multi-session Claude recovery, operators cannot disambiguate which cached session to resume even though the implementation was written for that path.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1139:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/z_archive/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/008-hydra-db-based-features/research/iterations/iteration-001-opus.md:36:9. **Bug: `validateGovernedIngest` returns `allowed: true` when no governance is needed, but normalizes empty strings for required fields** — When `requiresGovernedIngest` returns false (line 224-241), the function returns `allowed: true` with `tenantId: ''` and `sessionId: ''`. Downstream code that checks `decision.normalized.tenantId || null` (line 279 in `buildGovernancePostInsertFields`) will write `null` for these, but code checking `decision.normalized.tenantId` as truthy will incorrectly treat the empty string as present. The `Required<Pick<...>>` type forces these to be strings, hiding the emptiness.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2304:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/002-spec-kit-internals/003-template-levels/002-manifest-driven-template-design/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:28:| `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | JSON config fields: `resource_map.emit` or `resourceMap.emit`, `topic`, `createdAt`, `status`, `maxIterations`, `lineage.sessionId`, `lineage.parentSessionId`, `lineage.lineageMode`, `lineage.generation`, `lineage.continuedFromRun`. State JSONL fields: `type`, `iteration`/`run`, `newInfoRatio`, `status`, `focus`, `focusTrack`, `findingsCount`, `timestamp`, `answeredQuestions`, `sourcesQueried`, `convergenceSignals.compositeStop`, and event fields such as `event`, `signals`, `decision`, `blockers`. It reads no YAML frontmatter. | Strategy anchors replaced exactly as `<!-- ANCHOR:${anchorId} -->...<!-- /ANCHOR:${anchorId} -->` for `key-questions`, `answered-questions`, `what-worked`, `what-failed`, `exhausted-approaches`, `ruled-out-directions`, and `next-focus`. | Missing config/state/strategy files hard fail via `readFileSync`. Corrupt JSONL hard fails unless `--lenient`. Missing iteration directory is allowed as empty. Missing strategy anchor hard throws. Missing delta files only skips resource-map emission. | Iteration markdown parser expects exact second-level headings: `## Focus`, `## Findings`, `## Ruled Out`, `## Dead Ends`, `## Sources Consulted`, `## Reflection`, and `## Recommended Next Focus`. Strategy question parser expects `## 3. KEY QUESTIONS (remaining)` with `- [ ]` or `- [x]` items. Reflection labels are exact bullets: `What worked and why`, `What did not work and why`, `What I would do differently`. |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7853:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:102:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/fallback-router.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/prompt-pack.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/bayesian-scorer.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/session-state-hierarchy.cjs\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/round-state-jsonl.cjs\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/cost-guards.cjs\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/tests/.gitkeep\n\n(Results .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7877:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2806:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/review/hs/logs/iter-009.out:43:{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1780205290663,"sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_function_4vnhn0n98zpe_1","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md"},"output":"<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: ---\n2: name: deep-loop-runtime\n3: version: 1.4.0\n4: description: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\n5: allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n6: ---\n7: \n8: # Deep Loop Runtime\n9: \n10: Shared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n11: \n12: ---\n13: \n14: ## 1. WHEN TO USE\n15: \n16: This skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n17: \n18: Use it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n19: \n20: - executor configuration and audit metadata\n21: - prompt-pack rendering\n22: - post-dispatch validation\n23: - atomic state files and JSONL repair\n24: - loop locking and permissions gates\n25: - Bayesian scoring and fallback routing\n26: - coverage-graph schema, query, and signal interpretation\n27: \n28: ### Activation Triggers\n29: \n30: This skill is activated when:\n31: \n32: - A deep-review or deep-research workflow YAML invokes `node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/<name>.cjs`\n33: - A test file imports `from '.../deep-loop-runtime/lib/...'`\n34: - An operator queries / mutates the coverage graph via the runtime scripts\n35: - A workflow needs Bayesian convergence scoring or fallback executor routing\n36: \n37: ### When NOT to Use\n38: \n39: Do not invoke this skill when:\n40: \n41: - A user requests an interactive command (use `/deep:start-review-loop` or `/deep:start-research-loop` instead — those orchestrate this runtime)\n42: - A package outside deep-review / deep-research needs deep-loop semantics (out of supported scope; open a packet to extend ownership)\n43: - An MCP tool surface is needed (this skill explicitly has none — the isolation ADR removed the deep-loop MCP tools)\n44: \n45: ---\n46: \n47: ## 2. SMART ROUTING\n48: \n49: This skill has no direct LLM-facing keyword triggers — it is invoked by workflow YAMLs (via `bash:` script calls) and by test imports. The router below documents which sub-domain to load for a given consumer query.\n50: \n51: ### Primary Detection Signal\n52: \n53: ```bash\n54: request_text=\"$(printf '%s' \"$USER_REQUEST\" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')\"\n55: case \"$request_text\" in\n56:   *\"deep-loop runtime\"*|*\"deep-loop-runtime\"*|*\"executor config\"*|*\"prompt pack\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n57:   *\"coverage graph\"*|*\"deep_loop_graph\"*|*\"bayesian scorer\"*|*\"loop lock\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n58:   *\"fallback router\"*|*\"post-dispatch validate\"*|*\"jsonl repair\"*) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=1 ;;\n59:   *) DEEP_LOOP_INTENT=0 ;;\n60: esac\n61: ```\n62: \n63: ### Phase Detection\n64: \n65: ```text\n66: TASK CONTEXT\n67:     |\n68:     +- STEP 0: Detect runtime-call intent vs. consumer-orchestration intent\n69:     +- STEP 1: If consumer (workflow YAML), invoke the relevant scripts/*.cjs\n70:     +- STEP 2: If contributor (modifying lib code), load matching lib/ subtree\n71:     +- STEP 3: If test author, load tests/ + lib/ pair\n72: ```\n73: \n74: ### Resource Domains\n75: \n76: ```text\n77: lib/deep-loop/*.ts            # 10 runtime modules (executor, prompt-pack, validation, state, scoring, routing)\n78: lib/coverage-graph/*.ts       # 3 schema + query + signals modules\n79: scripts/*.cjs                 # 4 entry points (convergence, upsert, query, status)\n80: database/*.sqlite              # runtime-owned SQLite (deep-loop-graph.sqlite)\n81: tests/{unit,integration,lifecycle}/  # 21+ vitest files split by responsibility\n82: ```\n83: \n84: ### Resource Loading Levels\n85: \n86: | Tier | When loaded | Purpose |\n87: |------|-------------|---------|\n88: | ALWAYS | SKILL.md frontmatter | description, allowed-tools, version |\n89: | CONDITIONAL | When a runtime call dispatches | `lib/` modules required by the called function |\n90: | ON_DEMAND | When a test fails or a contributor extends | `tests/` + matching `lib/` sub-tree |\n91: \n92: ### Smart Router Pseudocode\n93: \n94: ```text\n95: IF request mentions a specific lib name (e.g. \"bayesian-scorer\", \"prompt-pack\"):\n96:   → Load lib/deep-loop/<name>.ts + matching tests/unit/<name>.vitest.ts\n97: ELIF request mentions coverage-graph / convergence / signals:\n98:   → Load lib/coverage-graph/*.ts + tests/integration/review-depth-*.vitest.ts\n99: ELIF request mentions a script (convergence.cjs, status.cjs, etc.):\n100:   → Load scripts/<name>.cjs + tests/integration/<name>-script.vitest.ts + lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts\n101: ELSE:\n102:   → Defer to consumer skill (deep-review / deep-research)\n103: ```\n104: \n105: ---\n106: \n107: ## 3. HOW IT WORKS\n108: \n109: ### Runtime Architecture\n110: \n111: The runtime exposes deep-loop primitives to consumer workflows through two paths: **direct `.cjs` script invocation** (replacing the removed `mcp__mk_spec_memory__deep_loop_graph_*` MCP tools) and **TypeScript library imports** (from workflow scripts and tests).\n112: \n113: ```text\n114: [deep-review/deep-research workflow YAML]\n115:             │\n116:             ├─ bash: node .../scripts/<name>.cjs   (coverage-graph mutations)\n117:             │       └─→ lib/coverage-graph/*.ts → database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite\n118:             │\n119:             └─ ts-import: lib/deep-loop/<lib>.ts   (runtime primitives)\n120:                     └─→ atomic-state, prompt-pack, post-dispatch-validate, etc.\n121: ```\n122: \n123: ### Library Structure\n124: \n125: **`lib/deep-loop/` (10 modules):**\n126: - `executor-config.ts` — schema + parsing for per-iteration executor settings\n127: - `executor-audit.ts` — appends `executor` block to iteration JSONL (provenance)\n128: - `prompt-pack.ts` — renders the iteration prompt template\n129: - `post-dispatch-validate.ts` — validates iteration outputs (markdown + JSONL + delta)\n130: - `atomic-state.ts` — atomic state-log writes\n131: - `jsonl-repair.ts` — recovers corrupt JSONL state lines\n132: - `loop-lock.ts` — single-writer locking\n133: - `permissions-gate.ts` — permission scope checks\n134: - `bayesian-scorer.ts` — convergence scoring\n135: - `fallback-router.ts` — executor fallback decision matrix\n136: \n137: **`lib/coverage-graph/` (3 modules):**\n138: - `coverage-graph-db.ts` — SQLite schema + node-kind allow-list + connection lifecycle\n139: - `coverage-graph-query.ts` — query builders\n140: - `coverage-graph-signals.ts` — convergence signal extraction\n141: \n142: ### Council Primitives\n143: \n144: The Runtime Boundary Decision (ADR-001) extends `deep-loop-runtime/lib/` with council-compatible infrastructure primitives while keeping operator-facing and domain workflow semantics in `deep-ai-council`. These primitives are consumed by downstream deep-ai-council orchestration (per-topic multi-round, session-findings registry, command and skill wiring, parity and cost docs) for per-topic orchestration, multi-topic session state, command wiring, parity tests and docs.\n145: \n146: **`lib/council/` (5 modules):**\n147: - `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs` — runs seat executors in parallel for one council round, preserves seat result order, and returns fulfilled/rejected per-seat outcomes plus round summary counts.\n148: - `round-state-jsonl.cjs` — appends per-round JSONL records with a lock-file single-writer guard, repairs corrupt trailing JSONL before append, fsyncs writes, and exposes round-state readers for resume.\n149: - `adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs` — scores Round-N -> Round-N+1 adjudicator verdict deltas using ADR-003 weights for option change, confidence delta, material-risk Jaccard delta, axis flip rate, and blocking-disagreement delta.\n150: - `cost-guards.cjs` — normalizes and enforces ADR-004 defaults for `max_rounds_per_topic`, `max_topics_per_session`, `saturation_threshold`, and `seats_per_round`, including upper-bound seat-output calculation.\n151: - `session-state-hierarchy.cjs` — creates and validates the ADR-002 session -> topic -> round state shape, including stable `topic-NNN-slug` and `round-NNN` ids.\n152: \n153: The existing `lib/deep-loop/atomic-state.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/jsonl-repair.ts`, and `lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts` remain the sibling runtime contracts. Council modules mirror those durability semantics in a council-scoped CJS surface so downstream `deep-ai-council` phases can consume them without modifying deep-review or deep-research behavior.\n154: \n155: ### Script Entry Points\n156: \n157: Four `.cjs` scripts replace the 4 deleted MCP tools, each honoring the same JSON-in / JSON-out contract documented in ADR-001 (the script interface contract):\n158: \n159: | Script | Replaces | Purpose | Exit codes |\n160: |--------|----------|---------|------------|\n161: | `scripts/convergence.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_convergence` | Computes typed CONTINUE / STOP_ALLOWED / STOP_BLOCKED decisions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n162: | `scripts/upsert.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_upsert` | Stores nodes + edges from iteration `graphEvents` | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n163: | `scripts/query.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_query` | Inspects uncovered questions, unverified claims, contradictions | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n164: | `scripts/status.cjs` | `deep_loop_graph_status` | Session-scoped health report | 0=ok, 1=script, 2=DB, 3=input |\n165: | `scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` | n/a (new) | Concurrency-capped worker pool + status ledger for fan-out lineages | exports only (no main) |\n166: | `scripts/fanout-run.cjs` | n/a (new) | CLI lineage pool driver — spawns N headless CLI subprocesses (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin), each running the full loop in `lineages/{label}/`; salvage sweep via fanout-salvage.cjs after each subprocess exits | 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed |\n167: | `scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs` | n/a (new) | Write-failure salvage: recovers missing iteration .md files from captured subprocess stdout; per-sessionId isolation preserved | exports only (no main) |\n168: | `scripts/fanout-merge.cjs` | n/a (new) | Cross-lineage merge: research (dedup by id + attribution) or review (strongest-restriction P0 rollup) → consolidated registry + fanout-attribution.md | 0=ok, 3=input |\n169: \n170: **Fan-out isolation invariant:** each CLI lineage receives its own `{base}/lineages/{label}/` artifact dir (via `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override in `step_resolve_artifact_root`) and its own `session_id` for `convergence.cjs` + coverage-graph DB writes (PK includes session_id — no schema change, no collision). Same-kind replicas get distinct `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` values to prevent lockfile contention.\n171: \n172: Each script: parses argv → opens `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` → calls the lib function → writes JSON to stdout → closes DB in `finally` → exits with the appropriate code.\n173: \n174: ### Test Organization\n175: \n176: ```text\n177: tests/\n178: ├── unit/         # 13 files — per-module unit tests (atomic-state, executor-config, etc.)\n179: ├── integration/  # 7 files — script invocations + review-depth fixtures\n180: ├── lifecycle/    # 1 file — db-open-close pattern verification\n181: └── _helpers/     # 1 file — spawn-cjs helper for direct-invocation tests\n182: ```\n183: \n184: The system-spec-kit `mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` so a single vitest run discovers both surfaces.\n185: \n186: ---\n187: \n188: ## 4. RULES\n189: \n190: ### ALWAYS\n191: \n192: - **ALWAYS** open the SQLite DB inside a `try` block and close it in a `finally` block (script lifecycle invariant per ADR-001, the script interface contract)\n193: - **ALWAYS** emit JSON-only output to stdout from `scripts/*.cjs` (workflow YAML parses stdout for output bindings)\n194: - **ALWAYS** include `'use strict';` on line 2 of every `.cjs` script (per sk-code OPENCODE JS style)\n195: - **ALWAYS** include a `MODULE: <name>` header comment in every `lib/**/*.ts` file (per sk-code OPENCODE TS-MODULE-HEADER convention)\n196: - **ALWAYS** preserve atomic-state semantics when mutating JSONL state — never partial-write\n197: - **ALWAYS** acquire `loop-lock` before any state-log mutation\n198: \n199: ### NEVER\n200: \n201: - **NEVER** register MCP tools from this skill — the isolation ADR explicitly removed the MCP surface; reintroducing tools defeats the FULL_ISOLATE direction\n202: - **NEVER** open the SQLite DB outside `lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts` — single owner of the connection\n203: - **NEVER** import from `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/` or `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/` — those locations are empty after the runtime moved here; import from this skill instead\n204: - **NEVER** write to `database/deep-loop-graph.sqlite` without holding the `loop-lock`\n205: - **NEVER** swallow exceptions in script entry points — propagate to the exit-code matrix\n206: \n207: ### ESCALATE IF\n208: \n209: - **ESCALATE** to the user if a runtime call requires a new MCP tool — the architectural direction prohibits MCP additions; the user must decide on policy override\n210: - **ESCALATE** to a new ownership ADR if a new consumer skill (beyond deep-review and deep-research) needs deep-loop runtime — extension requires a new ownership ADR\n211: - **ESCALATE** if the SQLite schema requires a backward-incompatible change — coordinate with all consumer workflows + add migration to `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n212: - **ESCALATE** if a script's stdout JSON contract changes — every workflow YAML output binding depends on the existing shape\n213: \n214: ---\n215: \n216: ## 5. SCOPE\n217: \n218: In scope:\n219: \n220: - runtime libraries under `lib/deep-loop/`\n221: - coverage-graph runtime libraries under `lib/coverage-graph/`\n222: - `.cjs` script entry points under `scripts/`\n223: - runtime-owned SQLite storage under `database/`\n224: - runtime tests under `tests/`\n225: \n226: Out of scope:\n227: \n228: - MCP tool registration\n229: - MCP handler ownership\n230: - DB lifecycle inside `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`\n231: - direct user command routing\n232: \n233: ---\n234: \n235: ## 6. ARCHITECTURE\n236: \n237: - `lib/deep-loop/` holds shared deep-loop runtime libraries.\n238: - `lib/coverage-graph/` holds coverage-graph schema, query, and signal helpers.\n239: - `scripts/` holds `.cjs` entry points used by workflow YAML and collateral commands.\n240: - `database/` owns relocated runtime SQLite artifacts.\n241: - `tests/` holds runtime-owned tests split from the MCP server surface.\n242: \n243: Shipped layout:\n244: \n245: | Path | Populated By | Purpose |\n246: |------|--------------|---------|\n247: | `lib/deep-loop/` | 10 files | Shared executor config, audit, prompt-pack, validation, state, repair, locking, permissions, scoring, and fallback routing. |\n248: | `lib/coverage-graph/` | 3 files | Coverage-graph schema owner, query helpers, and signal interpretation. |\n249: | `scripts/` | 4 files | Direct `status`, `query`, `upsert`, and `convergence` entry points replacing the removed deep-loop MCP tools. |\n250: | `database/` | SQLite | Runtime-owned `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. |\n251: | `tests/` | Unit, integration, lifecycle | Runtime-owned tests discovered by the system-spec-kit MCP server Vitest config. |\n252: \n253: ---\n254: \n255: ## 7. INTEGRATION POINTS\n256: \n257: - **ADR-001 (AI Council SPLIT ruling)**: original AI Council SPLIT ruling. Superseded by user directive.\n258: - **Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP)**: Moves deep-loop runtime, coverage-graph schema/query/signals, script entry points, storage, and tests into this peer skill while removing the deep-loop MCP tool surface.\n259: - **Deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML** call this runtime through script entry points after the runtime cutover.\n260: - **system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts** includes `'../deep-loop-runtime/tests/**/*.{vitest,test}.ts'` for cross-package test discovery.\n261: - **deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs** depends on the moved coverage-graph runtime via lib imports.\n262: \n263: ---\n264: \n265: ## 8. SUCCESS CRITERIA\n266: \n267: The runtime is healthy when:\n268: \n269: - Every `scripts/` entry point (`status`, `query`, `upsert`, `convergence`) returns valid JSON on its documented stdout contract\n270: - Consumer workflows resolve their `lib/` imports without path errors\n271: - The runtime-owned SQLite under `database/` is present and its coverage-graph schema matches `coverage-graph-db.ts`\n272: - Runtime tests under `tests/` pass through the system-spec-kit Vitest discovery glob\n273: \n274: ---\n275: \n276: ## 9. REFERENCES AND RELATED RESOURCES\n277: \n278: - ADR-001 (script interface contract): documents the script-shim and DB-relocation contract.\n279: - Isolation ADR (FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP): documents the MCP tool surface removal rationale.\n280: - Decision rationale lives in this skill's changelog and originating decision records.\n281: \n282: Related resources:\n283: \n284: - Consumer skills: the `deep-review` and `deep-research` workflows call this runtime through the script entry points\n285: - `system-spec-kit/mcp_server/vitest.config.ts` discovers this skill's `tests/`\n286: - Per-release detail and rationale: this skill's `changelog/` directory\n\n(End of file - total 286 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"---\nname: deep-loop-runtime\nversion: 1.4.0\ndescription: \"Shared deep-loop runtime: executor + prompt-pack + validation + atomic state + coverage-graph + Bayesian scoring + fallback routing.\"\nallowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Glob, Grep]\n---\n\n# Deep Loop Runtime\n\nShared runtime infrastructure for the deep-review and deep-research loop workflows. The FULL_ISOLATE_NO_MCP consolidation moved the shared runtime libraries, coverage-graph ownership, script entry points, SQLite storage, and runtime tests into this peer skill.\n\n---\n\n## 1. WHEN TO USE\n\nThis skill is consumed by deep-review and deep-research workflow YAML and script paths, not invoked directly as a user-facing workflow.\n\nUse it when a deep-loop workflow needs shared runtime support for:\n\n- executor configuration and audit metadata","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md","time":{"start":1780205290639,"end":1780205290662}},"id":"prt_e7c81029a0018WgctXtXLcmFh5","sessionID":"ses_1838070fafferbmuIbMyAqlS9c","messageID":"msg_e7c80eee8001ZiIO6I4pjCr85X"}}

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7884:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3868:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:33:      "title": "Sole concurrency test exercises the wx fresh-lease path, not the DR-002-01 reclaim re-read CAS",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7885:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3869:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:34:      "why": "The DR-002-01 fix added a re-read-after-write CAS to the RECLAIM branch of acquireOwnerLeaseFile (mk-code-index-launcher.cjs lines 373-386), which runs only when an `existing` stale owner lease is present. The only concurrency regression test, 'lets exactly one concurrent launcher own the lease' (line 207), spawns two launchers from a fresh createWorkspace() (tmpdir, no seeded owner lease, line 24-42). With no `existing` lease, both launchers take the `writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive` (O_EXCL `wx`) fresh path (lines 360-371), whose single-winner guarantee comes entirely from O_EXCL and predates DR-002-01. The reclaim-path re-read CAS (the actual code DR-002-01 changed) is never reached: 'reclaims a stale-heartbeat owner lease with a live PID' (line 309) and 'reclaims a dead-pid lease' (line 296) each spawn only ONE launcher against a pre-seeded stale lease, so no two processes ever concurrently hit the reclaim branch. The test therefore passes identically against the un-fixed code (delete lines 377-384 and 'exactly one concurrent launcher' still goes green), giving false confidence that the reclaim CAS is verified.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7886:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3870:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:142:      "title": "Owner-lease state machine is duplicated across launcher.cjs and owner-lease.ts with DIVERGENT concurrency primitives",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7887:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4013:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/011-research-post-stress-finding-followups/research/deltas/iter-007.jsonl:1:{"type":"iteration","iteration":7,"newInfoRatio":0.34,"status":"completed","focus":"Status snapshot closure and P0/P1 contradiction check","questionsCovered":["Q-P0","Q-P1","Q-P2","Q-OPP","Q-ARCH"],"sourcesRead":[".opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md",".opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/014-mcp-runtime-stress-remediation/011-post-stress-finding-remediation-research/research/deep-research-strategy.md",".opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/014-mcp-runtime-stress-remediation/011-post-stress-finding-remediation-research/research/iterations/iteration-005.md",".opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/014-mcp-runtime-stress-remediation/011-post-stress-finding-remediation-research/research/iterations/iteration-006.md",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/handlers/status.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/handlers/query.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/handlers/README.md",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/lib/ensure-ready.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/lib/readiness-contract.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/file-watcher.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/code-graph-query-fallback-decision.vitest.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/ensure-ready.vitest.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/deep-loop/executor-config.vitest.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/tests/code-graph-siblings-readiness.vitest.ts"],"keyFindings":["Q-P0 winner remains command-owned Copilot target authority because resolveCopilotPromptArg still only chooses raw prompt versus @PROMPT_PATH and has no approved-folder or plan-only Gate 3 contract.","Q-P1 winner remains an isolated SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR degraded-graph harness because unit coverage already exercises all fallbackDecision branches while the stress gap was end-to-end degraded state.","Q-P2 winner is a new non-mutating getGraphReadinessSnapshot helper for code_graph_status; getGraphFreshness currently loses action/reason detail, while ensureCodeGraphReady is unsuitable because it caches, cleans deleted tracked files, and can index.","Do not lower file-watcher debounce first; existing debounce/write-stability/concurrency/dedupe behavior makes status action visibility the lower-risk first fix.","Q-ARCH should carry target-authority vs recovered-context and readiness-action vs freshness-label as the two scoped seams."],"timestamp":"2026-04-27T17:22:17Z"}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7964:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:2788:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/gpt55/iterations/iteration-001.md:107:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:2766:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/007-mcp-daemon-reliability/011-deep-review-shutdown-and-codegraph/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:49:Covered the full close + WAL checkpoint lifecycle in vector-index-store.ts: close_db (1286-1311), checkpointAllWal (1313-1317), detachActiveVectorShard (804-814), attachActiveVectorShard ordering (749-798), the db_connections map (declared line 682, set at 1273, cleared at 1297), set_active_database_connection (693-729), and initialize_db close paths (1241/1254/1266). Verified barrel re-exports in vector-index.ts (lines 129-169) correctly surface close_db/checkpointAllWal/detachActiveVectorShard with no shadowing or missing exports. Confirmed no double-close of the active db (loop skips conn===db, then the if(db) block closes it) and no double-close on validation-failure paths (those connections are closed inline and never added to db_connections, which only happens after all validation passes at line 1273). Examined the checkpointAllWal/close_db TOCTOU on module-global `db`: NOT recorded as a finding because Node's single-threaded JS model with no await boundaries inside either function precludes real interleaving, and both pragmas are best-effort try/catch wrapped. COULD NOT verify at runtime: (1) whether better-sqlite3 DETACH actually leaves a non-empty shard -wal in practice (SQLite-version dependent) -- CORR-002 rests on the code's own documented TRUNCATE rationale, not a reproduced corruption; (2) actual call sites/concurrency of checkpointAllWal vs close_db from the daemon watchdog/signal handlers (the 007-mcp-daemon-reliability caller layer was out of scope and not read); (3) whether multi-path db_connections (>1 tracked connection) ever co-exist at runtime, which gates the severity of CORR-001/002/003 -- if only one connection is ever live, all three drop toward P2/non-issue.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:2788:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/122-deep-improvement-skill-benchmark-mode/006-deep-review/gpt55/iterations/iteration-001.md:107:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:2824:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/003-cross-cutting-cleanup-pass/024-daemon-concurrency-fixes/spec.md:64:Packet 046 deep research iteration-001 identified four concurrency defects in the skill-advisor daemon and freshness layer. Three are P1 (watcher flush reentrancy, lifecycle/shutdown ordering, generation lock-token theft) and one is P2 (out-of-order cache invalidation). Each defect lets the daemon's persisted state diverge from reality under realistic timer + shutdown + GC-pause interleavings.

Without it, concurrent saves to the same anchor would race.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:4231:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/002-spec-kit-internals/002-skill-advisor/001-skill-graph/002-skill-graph-daemon-native-advisor-tools/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:13:- `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/file-watcher.ts:299`-`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/file-watcher.ts:405` → Reindex scheduling resolves real paths, verifies containment, hashes content, skips unchanged files, bounds concurrency, retries `SQLITE_BUSY`, and records timing metrics.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:4232:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/002-spec-kit-internals/002-skill-advisor/001-skill-graph/002-skill-graph-daemon-native-advisor-tools/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:45:The most important A1 design pressure is editor save behavior. Vim explicitly documents rename-and-new-file save modes that affect watcher daemons (`https://vimhelp.org/options.txt.html:L1064-L1101`), while Chokidar offers `atomic` and `awaitWriteFinish` knobs for atomic and chunked write patterns (`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/paulmillr/chokidar/main/README.md:L8-L19`). The repo's current generic helper already combines `awaitWriteFinish`, content hashing, containment checks, bounded concurrency, `SQLITE_BUSY` retry, delete cleanup, and event mapping (`file-watcher.ts:251-258`, `file-watcher.ts:299-405`, `file-watcher.ts:407-445`), and its tests cover delete, rename, burst rename, concurrent rename, debounce, timing, and retry cases (`file-watcher.vitest.ts:304-606`).
